name: Assistant Manager - Application Technology Skin Rinse Off
description:

We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. (A) Core Purpose Develop rinse-off application technology based on business needs and contribute to build up identified technical competences and the necessary know how for South Asia market and beyond. Contribute to regional technical support and preparation of promotion kits for rinse-off product portfolio. Implement key customer projects in rinse-off application (B) Main Responsibilities, Tasks & Deliverables Be responsible for implementation of application technology development in rinse-off category. Carry out marketing concept and solutions development in collaboration with local and regional market development teams. Be responsible for rinse-off technical support and key customer projects implementation in South Asia Communicate/exchange application technical know-how within region. Transfer the new application technology and new products launch from global to local Support internal or external seminars / workshop / trainings and events Documentation and technical data maintenance and summaries. Comply with lab safety, health and environment. (C) Key Interfaces G-EMP/R Global Development Projects Leaders G-EMP Regional/Local Market Development Team Local Sales Team Analytical team at BASF Innovation Campus Mumbai (D) Performance Measures New concept and application of new products according to identified rinse-off market needs Successfully implementation of marketing concepts and key customer projects Maintenance of lab instruments and safe working environment and support AT laboratory development following BASF EHS compliance (E) Key Competencies (G= General; L=Leadership) Demonstrate Customer Focus: L Communicate Effectively: G Collaborate for Achievement: G (F) Technical Competencies Technical & Professional Knowledge: Chemistry / Applied Chemistry / Physical Chemistry / Cosmetics Formulation Experience, instrumental analysis, laboratory safety management, standard IT-applications (G) Minimum Education and Qualification Requirement Education: BSc or MSc in chemistry, cosmetic science or related area Working experience: MSc or BSc with 5+ years of working experience in a laboratory environment. Other requirements: English- Fluent in reading, writing, and speaking Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a

description:
Credit Risk Management (Associate Analyst) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English Risk Management (Associate Analyst) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Objectives of the Position Responsible for customer's credit risk management activities for the assigned market portfolio based on market principle and CCRM Services Hub Asia Pacific scope, following global and regional guidelines To ensure deliveries meeting requirements defined in service level agreements and KPIs for process efficiency, effectiveness and quality control Support Assistant Manager / Manager in day-to day service delivery and talent identification Main Tasks Functional Tasks (including but not limited to) Monitor customer master data creation and changes which relevant to CCRM (example payment terms) via SCO Checking regularly that customer portfolio is up-to-date, identifying inactive customers as well as carrying out deactivations Perform risk assessment, mitigation measures, recommendations and documentation by using CCRM tools Decide on internal risk rating, credit limit, order processing code (for medium and higher threshold of exposures applicable for Senior Analyst and Senior Specialist) or obtain necessary approval based on approval matrix Review and decide on the release of blocked orders or obtaining necessary approvals based on approval matrix and delegation to Hub Monitor credit risk of customers (limit usage, BASF Risk Rating, sales, etc.) Work closely with Analyst, Senior Specialist, Assistant Manager and Manager in daily operations and targets initiates appropriate measures to achieve defined objectives and target Provide necessary reporting for country, regional, and global timely in compliance with reporting requirements Following up of outstanding invoices and unresolved dispute items in case support needed by Collection team Coordinate, attend queries, and provide transparency to stakeholders on credit risk management core processes Perform other CCRM processes applicable for the assigned countries such as IFRS9 review, prolongation, bad debt, etc Act as Area of Competency (AOC) member or topic owner for CCRM related processes Project Management Support in steering Internal, Regional and Global processes implementation in Asia Pacific Region Support Asia Pacific’s business requirement / variation from Global / Regional process, preparation of business case and escalate when necessary Develop working relationships with the local companies and project owners to ensure that project solutions are adopted, implemented, and maintained appropriately Assist to communicate project deliverables to ensure all stakeholders are kept informed on progress and issues/resolutions Compliance Participate in any compliance audits and respond to audit queries (for both internal and external) Ensure all work documentation and work processes are updated and met with BASF policies & guideline, local company’s policy, legal and statutory requirements Quality and Continuous Improvement Identify, recommend, and implement continuous improvement opportunities in quality and efficiency to support smooth implementation Prepare and review training materials and conduct end user trainings when required Support new and existing team members through proper knowledge transfer; and sharing of best practice thereby ensuring quality is maintained through any staff transition Perform sample check activities for order release and risk assessment; to update issue log with detail root cause, preventive action and provide resolution Others Support Business Continuity Plan (BCP) through resource planning, backup and communication Any other tasks due to Service Hub KL management strategy and/ or changing business environment may require to be performed Additionally, for Senior Specialist Act as coordinator of cluster for certain countries, projects, and major process expertise Managing escalation (first level) from service recipients and escalate as and when required Participate or contribute to Business Unit strategy / business steering discussions in areas of key customer management, credit risk management and early warning signals Provide financial consultancy to customer whenever required Review monitoring and reporting done by Analyst / Associate Analyst / Senior Analyst whenever required Requirements Education Bachelor’s Degree or Diploma in Finance/ Accounting or related disciplines or Professional qualification in Finance (e.g., ACCA, CPA, etc.) Working Experience & Skills 2 years’ of experience in Finance and Accounting. Fresh Graduates are encouraged to apply. Diploma in Finance or Accounting background. Have market knowledge / expertise for Japan market and language proficiency is a plus Strong interpersonal skills to communicate to service recipients Excellent time management, good organization skills and multi-tasking skills Ability to work independently with minimal supervision, and to excel under pressure for meeting any tight deadlines Self-motivated, hardworking, and with management skills Curiosity to learn, adaptability to changes and agile and ability to challenge status quo Technical & Professional Knowledge Knowledge of MS Office Applications, especially MS Word, Excel, and Power Point Knowledge of SAP Systems Proficiency in English and other languages required Proficient knowledge and understanding on related processes Knowledge in programing or scripting are an added advantage but not mandatory The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com . Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a

name: Senior Analyst - Invoice to Cash
description:
Senior Analyst - Invoice to Cash Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Senior Analyst - Invoice to Cash LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Objectives of the Position •    Independently manage operational processing for Invoice-to-Cash (ITC) services, ensuring compliance with BASF requirements, Financial Reporting Compliance (FRC), and local statutory regulations •    Act as reviewer and approver for daily and month-end tasks, ensuring accuracy and timeliness •    Support Assistant Manager/Manager in service delivery, talent development, and process improvement initiatives •    Drive continuous improvement, digitalisation, and harmonisation across countries in the Asia Pacific region Main Tasks Functional Tasks (Inclusive but not limited): •    Process and review ITC workflows, including Accounts Receivable, Collections, Treasury Back Office and Intercompany Reconciliation activities •    Supervise and mentor Analysts, ensuring adherence to predefined process description and service management framework •    Troubleshoot and resolve queries from internal and external stakeholders promptly •    Act as Area of Competency (AOC) member or topic owner for ITC-related processes •    Monitor unallocated receipts, overdue accounts, and post-dunning activities; propose corrective actions •    To perform other duties as and when delegated Project Management: •    Support internal, regional, and global process implementation and transition projects •    Develop strong working relationships with local companies and project owners to ensure successful adoption of solutions •    Communicate project deliverables and progress to stakeholders Compliance: •    Participate in any compliance audits and respond to audit queries (for both internal and external) •    Ensure all work documentation and work processes are updated and met with BASF policies & guideline, local company’s policy, legal and statutory requirements Quality and Continuous Improvement: •    Identify, recommend, and implement continuous improvement opportunities in quality and efficiency to support smooth implementation •    Prepare and review training materials and conduct end user trainings when required •    Support new and existing team members through proper knowledge transfer; and sharing of best practice thereby ensuring quality is maintained through any staff transition Others: •    Support Business Continuity Plan (BCP) through resource planning, backup and communication •    Any other tasks due to Service Hub KL management strategy and/ or changing business environment may require to be performed Additionally, for Senior Associate Analyst: •    For development purposes, any additional task can be assigned as aligned Minimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position Education: •    University degree in Finance, Accounting, Economics or Business Administration; and/or •    Chartered or Certified Professional Accountant or equivalent Working Experience: •    Minimum 3 years’ experience in Finance & Accounting or Controlling function •    Diploma in Finance or Accounting with 4-5 years workings experience in related fields •    Experience in a Shared Services or Business Process Outsourcing engagement will be an advantage Technical & Professional Knowledge: •    Experience with Controlling/Finance tools and KPIs •    Experience working with SAP ERP and / or SAP BW tools is preferred •    Knowledge and experience of MS office tools. •    Proficiency in English and other languages as required with goo d communication skills. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. description:

Offer day-to-day support to service recipients on HR inquiries, work collaboratively with others and perform research on payroll and HR data practices by staying abreast of current and emerging market insights and legal legislations in local countries. Participate in process improvements, automation, and digitalization measures to offer best service delivery experience. Main Tasks Operational Excellence Ensure data quality and data integrity for Payroll and HR data processes. Receive, analyze, process and follow-up on personnel administration requests via multiple request channels, ensure accurate and timely interface to the respective internal/ external processing chains i.e. payroll, finance, data analytics, external service providers, local authorities etc. Manage entire termination workflow for all termination types which includes leaves calculations, information checks, letters preparation, systems updates, payment calculations and exit clearance. Responsible for time and attendance administrations, including manual calculations on time allowances/deductions, maintenance of work schedules, leaves, overtimes, and shift substitutions. Support the delivery of payroll inputs and administration i.e. employee subsidies, fleet management, long service awards etc. Support the salary review exercises on employee data preparation, letter generations and new salary processing. Responsible for the local labor administrative activities and scheduled statutory HR reporting, ensure timely and compliant deliveries as per legislation requirements. Responds to recurring or ad-hoc business needs to design and to deliver country specific or customized reports which could not be auto-generated by the analytics/reporting systems. Work closely with other teams and external service providers to ensure all service requests are completed accurately and timely. Service Engagement Providing advisory on employee enquiries, policy/guidelines clarifications, HR processes, HR systems guides, legislation processes etc. Monitor multiples service requests to ensure services delivered by the team are in accordance with established Service Level Agreement (SLA) and internal Key Performance Indicators (KPI). Perform research and information gathering on local market HR news, legislation updates and guidelines to maintain internal knowledge database. Analyze, clarify, and resolve operation issues and enquiries with close collaboration with relevant Country HR and Regional HR Business Partners to ensure quality services are delivered from the service hub. All unsolved and potential operational issues are escalated timely according to establish escalation process, with detailed analysis provided to Assistant Manager and Manager. Process Improvement and Project Initiatives Continuously review and update knowledge documents in areas of Payroll and People data to develop the formation of competence hub. Identify opportunities, prepare and analyze business case, and participate in digitalization and process optimization. Actively work on implementation of process improvements, automation and digitalization measures which will be rolled out predominantly in the GB KL Hub for achieving the efficiency target. Drive initiatives which contribute to the improvement of service experience Identify deviations from the established blueprints of multiple system processors in Payroll and HR data maintenance processes and escalate to Assistant Manager and Manager in ensuring the capturing of business requirements, system settings, configuration needs and change trackers. Provide inputs and participate in user acceptance testing in system maintenance and upgrade, follow up and escalate timely with respective party to ensure fixes or changes can be delivered on time. Team Collaboration Works with moderate supervision within established procedures and practices. Contribute positively to a knowledge sharing environment through generously sharing knowledge with others. Assist in orientation training and act as buddy to new team member. Job Requirements Education Relevant tertiary qualifications: Degree in Accounting, Mathematics, Commerce or Business Administration and Finance Work Experience With 1 - 2 years of related HR working experiences in Payroll and HR Operations Preferably experience in relevant HR procedures, practices, and country statutory Preferably working knowledge of integrated payroll systems preferably on SAP. Technical & Professional Knowledge Demonstrates a good level of proficiency in language and culture of the supported country/location. Preferably understanding of HR principals and/or Employee Laws of the supported country/location. Strong processing skills and analytical abilities (inclination for Mathematics/Finance/Problem solving). Service mindset with strong interpersonal and communications skills. Functional knowledge and skills in using Microsoft application i.e. Words, Excel, Power Point. About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. name: Deputy Manager- Technical Service - Personal Care
description:
Deputy Manager- Technical Service - Personal Care Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Deputy Manager- Technical Service - Personal Care LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Technical key account management and key interface of customers R&D Translate the customer needs into technical projects briefing Drive Technical service projects delivery with business impact How You Will Do It 1. Technical Interface & Application Development Serve as the gatekeeper for all R&D and technical exchanges between customers and BASF. Develop appropriate solutions in lab and present technical proposals, new concepts, and application ideas tailored to customer needs. Support implementation of these solutions at customer sites through trials, formulation guidance, and troubleshooting. Provide technical consulting, training, and hands-on support to customers. 2. Technical Service Strategy & Innovation Develop and execute proactive technical service strategies that enhance long-term customer loyalty. Identify customer-driven innovation opportunities and emerging application fields specific to Personal Care. Lead joint development projects with customers, ensuring strong project management and full accountability for deliverables. Manage and mitigate product and process risks, ensuring compliance with technical and regulatory standards. 3. Technical Key Account Management & Stakeholder Influence Influence key decision-makers within customer organizations and internally within BASF—even without direct authority—to drive technical and commercial progress. Build and maintain strong, strategic, long-term relationships with key accounts across R&D, procurement, marketing, operations, and leadership. Leverage in-depth knowledge of Personal Care formulations, applications, and market value chains to anticipate trends and guide customer development efforts. Act as the liaison between customer teams and internal BASF functions to ensure smooth communication and high customer satisfaction. 4. Capability Building Enhance the technical capability of the sales team and distributor teams through structured trainings, coaching, and joint customer visits. Promote strong cross-functional understanding of product performance and application behavior 5. EHS & Compliance Follow and promote BASF’s Environmental, Health, and Safety (EHS) practices during all technical service activities. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position This position aims to provide end-to-end delivery services by efficiently handling customer service and logistics management to maintain business continuity, enhance service level and safety continuity in business. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Manage end-to-end daily order process and operation By collaborating with multiple functions (internal and external) including but not limited to customers, sales, GB, suppliers, finance, controlling. The operation includes but not limited to: Sales order creation PO transaction. Payment following up and order release. Delivery schedule coordination. Delivery order creation. Shipment creation. Transportation arrangement. Billing and invoice management. Documents collection. Month end reconciliation. Import/export related coordination and operation. Monitor shipping and import/export status for overseas supply & delivery. Escalate any issues for on-time fulfillment. Capture market intelligence on supply chain related topics From daily interaction with customers and share with the business as appropriate, includes but not limited to: Provide best possible service to customers and promote the establishment of long-term customer relationships to ensure a sound profitable future customers base. Ensure best logistic cost execution through daily transportation management operation. Continuously improve end-to-end efficiency with creative ideas and digital tools’ support. Complete any unstated end-to-end officer-related tasks assigned by the manager. Manage customers’ complaints NCM operation and communication according to defined procedures / system. Escalate critical NCMs when needed for appropriate resolution. Operational Compliance Ensure that all operations (also at customer premises) are carried out in accordance with internal standards and local government regulations. Report any areas of concern immediately to Supply Chain Manager. Job Requirements Education: Bachelor’s degree or higher in International Trade, Logistics, Supply Chain Management, or a related field. Work Experience: Minimum 2–3 years of experience in order-to-cash processes; experience in the chemical industry is an advantage. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Language Skills: Proficient in Mandarin (both written and spoken) for communication with customers in China, as well as fluency in English. Technical Skills: Strong SAP competency is required. Personal Attributes: Thrives in a dynamic environment, maintains a positive and solutions‑oriented mindset, adapts well to change, collaborates effectively across teams, and consistently demonstrates strong professionalism and work ethic. description:

Ensure that timelines and quality meet the agreed service levels and targets. To support and provide guidance to Analysts in performing Financial Reporting tasks. To back up, assist with and perform Financial Reporting tasks as required. Main Tasks Functional Tasks (including but not limited to): Independently provide Financial Reporting transaction processing support and ensuring delivery quality in terms of accuracy, completeness and timeliness on daily basis. Perform and analyse booking of accounting transactions, fixed asset transactions, master data maintenance and account reconciliation including account clearing. Perform analytical review of profit and loss accounts and balance sheet, trend analysis and transaction posting checks. Prepare and coordinate for month-end, quarter-end, year-end and statutory financial reports for BASF companies across Asia Pacific on timely, accurate and complete basis. This would include reports for group reporting, internal management reporting, statutory reporting as well as any agreed ad hoc reports required e.g. audit schedules, tax schedules etc. Trouble shoot and liaise with relevant stakeholders to resolve issues and queries related to Financial Reporting processes. Contribute or participate in working groups (i.e. Smart Close, Final Sprint, RRM, CbCR & etc.) with specific expertise knowledge. Perform back up and other duties as and when required or delegated. Compliance and Quality: Participate in any compliance audits and respond to audit queries. Responsible in ensuring all work documentations and work processes are updated in compliance to BASF policies & guidelines, Financial Reporting Compliance requirements, local company legal and statutory requirements. Continuous Improvement and Projects: Identify, recommend and implement solutions for continuous improvement opportunities. Contribute and drive the implementation of project initiatives e.g. technology enhancement, internal control, merger and acquisition etc. Lead local projects and participate in regional projects. Knowledge Management: Contribute inputs to maintain and update a set of comprehensive and complete training materials of Financial Reporting functional training. Contribute to Financial Reporting internal control measures. Contribute to knowledge database by updating company specific procedures and issue logs (including resolution and preventive action) to facilitate continuous learning, development and knowledge retention. Support new and existing team members through effective knowledge transfer and sharing of best practice for sustainable development of knowledge within the team. Conduct functional training in accordance with established training structure (when necessary). Frequent collaboration with relevant stakeholders: Counterparts in BASF companies, internal/external auditors, external consultants and BSSCKL cross functional units. Specialists, Experts, Assistant Managers, Managers, Department Head, Analysts and peers. Corporate Finance unit. Global Financial Reporting functional units. Degree of Supervision Required in the job: Work with minimal supervision. Minimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position Education: University degree in finance and accounting or business studies; and/or Chartered or Certified Professional Accountant or equivalent. Working Experience: Minimum 3 years’ experiences in Financial Reporting or other accounting function. Competent candidates with lesser years of experience are considerable. Preferably experience working in SAP system or other major ERP system’s work environment. Experience in a shared service or business process outsourcing engagement will be an added advantage. Experience working in a MNC or Malaysian Public listed company preferred. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Knowledge and understanding of Financial Reporting and other accounting functions. Knowledge and experience of MS office tools. Proficiency in English and other language required with good communication skill. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Operational Excellence •    Responsible in preparation of data and analysis required to support any Compensation & Benefits initiatives such as performance management, salary budgeting and merit increment cycle, annual benefit renewal program and market benchmarking exercise for countries in APAC region •    Supports in collating economic indicators, external market practices and data analysis patterns and translates into meaningful indicators for development of any C&B initiatives and proposals to maintain market competitiveness • Compensation Planner, Gambit) implementation and takes into consideration the requirements and interactions with the Payroll & People Data team •    Provides support to country HR for utilization of responsiveness on ticketing system •    Provide Compensation & Benefits inquiry support to employees via HR4U / CRM •    Support the Analysts should there be an escalation of C&B matters that require We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About BASF At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises six segments: Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com . Objectives of the Position According to the defined production processes and quality requirements, you are required to schedule and plan in line with Company EHS guideline, properly operate the machines/equipment, and handle the chemical raw materials to fabricate the products that meet the requirements in quantity, quality, schedule, and process. How You Will Do It Main Tasks •    Perform on-site control and manual operation in the area of responsibility •    Maintain a clean environment in the area of responsibility •    All activities at work have to comply with the company's administration, safety, environmental protection and health regulations •    Comply and perform all operations in accordance with the statement of work for all production activities to ensure that the product quality meets the cGMP standard •    Undergo the assignment given by the operator in the control room and complete it •    Attend training(s) •    Handling and reporting of abnormal conditions On duty •    Handover: Include the current operation status, abnormal progress, maintenance status and unfinished work in the area of responsibility •    Perform environmental and equipment clean-up work in the area of responsibility •    Daily maintenance of equipment, automatic inspection (SAMACO) and fire safety equipment inspection •    Cooperate with the operator in the control room about sampling and all manual operations •    Manage and organize the tools within the area of responsibility •    Record the batch tables in production and perform on-site anomaly handling and reporting •    Perform the tasks as assigned by the superior/supervisor Inter-relationship with the relevant unit •    Take-out from the warehouse in conjunction description:

We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. 關於巴斯夫 在巴斯夫，我們創造化學新作用——追求永續發展的未來。我們的宏偉目標是成為客戶首選的化工公司，推動其綠色轉型。我們將經濟上的成功、社會責任和環境保護相結合。巴斯夫在全球約有112,000 名員工，為遍佈全球各類產業客戶的成功做出貢獻。我們的業務中，「核心業務」包括化學品、材料、工業解決方案、營養與護理業務領域，「自主業務」包括表面處理技術和農業解決方案領域。2024 年巴斯夫全球銷售額為 653 億歐元。巴斯夫的股票在法蘭克福證券交易所上市 (交易代號: BAS) ，並在美國發行存託憑證 (交易代號: BASFY)。欲了解更多資訊，請上網: www.basf.com 職位目標 根據定義的生產流程和品質要求，您需要按照公司EHS指南進行安排和計劃，正確操作機器/設備，並處理化學原料，以製造在數量，品質，進度和進程方面符合要求的產品。 職位職責 主要工作 •    執行責任區内現場的控制及手動操 •    維持責任區内環境整潔 •    當班之所有活動遵守公司行政、安全、環保、衛生之規定 •    遵守及執行製程生產中各項操作依照工作説明書規範進行所有生產活動，確保產品品質合cGMP規範 •    接受控制室操作員指派工作，並完成之 •    接受訓練 •    異常狀況處理及報告 當班工作執掌 •    交接班：内容含蓋責任區内的操作現況、異常經過、維修狀況及未完成的工作 •    執行責任區内環境、設備清理工作 •    設備日常保養、執行自動檢查（SAMACO) 及消防安全設備檢查 •    配合控制室操作員交代的取樣及所有的手動操作 •    管理責任區内所屬的工具，并整理之 •    記錄製程生產中的批次表，並執行現場異常狀況處理及報告 •    執行上級主管交辦事項 與相關單位之互相關係 •    配合生產中向倉庫領料 •    配合生產中送樣品至品管化驗室檢測 •    配合設備維修中，工安的檢查 EHS 檢測 •    確保製程區内的整潔、通道無阻塞及無危害的可能 •    保持工作場所和工作環境 •    保障工作安全和高效的生產活動 •    確保符合當地的安全消防相關法規，以及公司的EHS方針 •    執行快速意外事件（事故）RIR之立即通報 •    新進人員之督導 •    執行安全觀察AHA program（危害識別） •    落實執行TDS之規範 職位需求 •   大專及以上學歷，化學或工程相關專業畢 •   2年以上石化或化工廠相關工作經驗 •   瞭解 ISO 9001 和 GMP 質量體系 •   基本的英語讀/寫能力 •   基本的電腦操作功能、文書作業（例如 Word、Excel） •   具備堆高機駕照、起重機操作執照佳 •   三班輪班制 08:00-16:00,16:00-24:00, 24:00-08:00 立即應徵 獨特整體待遇 — you@BASF 巴斯夫不僅提供薪酬。我們所提供的整體待遇包括許多不同面向，在您人生的每一階段發揮最佳作用。我們稱之爲you@BASF。請按此以獲得更多資訊。 獨特整體待遇 — you@BASF 巴斯夫不僅提供薪酬。我們所提供的整體待遇包括許多不同面向，在您人生的每一階段發揮最佳作用。我們稱之爲you@BASF。請按此以獲得更多資訊。 At BASF, we believe that people are the key to our long-term success and that talent is in everyone. description:
Import Export Asia Pacific (Executive) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Import Export Asia Pacific (Executive) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Objectives of the Position Provide end-to-end operational support for import and export shipments across APAC, ensuring on-time execution, documentation accuracy, and compliance with global trade regulations. Act as the coordination hub between suppliers, logistics service providers , Business Units, and customs brokers to deliver reliable, compliant, and cost-effective shipping operations. Managing and maintenance of import export and customs operations, handling relevant administrative activities, reporting, and documentation processes for the assigned countries Archiving all applicable documentation in accordance with company and regulatory guidelines Proactively escalate cases of deviations in transportation activities to bring shipment back on track in meeting defined and agreed delivery dates Meet defined service levels and any agreed qualitative requirements pertaining to core transportation and documentation scope of work Support monitoring and evaluation of service provider’s performance referring to agreed KPIs and conduct periodic quality reviews Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Responsible for logistics fulfilment processes (including SAP TM maintenance and process adherence) for all import and export shipments by working closely with the country import export & customs teams and hub-based overseas delivery services community Responsible for analyzing issues and managing where possible, escalating to country and/or Hub import export & customs teams for attention or improvements required Non-conformances management (NCM) and issue log maintenance and ensure reported issues are addressed with corrective action (if applicable) Consolidate, prepare and verify import export & customs operational reports to management and other relevant stakeholders Participate in system and new process flow testing where required Initiate continuous improvement initiatives and issue resolution of supply chain issues pertaining inbound and outbound transportation management on behalf of assigned countries. Participation in import export community meetings / activities where required and support service provider management and service delivery performance in assigned countries from global services hub Other responsibilities All other tasks as assigned relevant to meeting BASF and the objectives as outlined above. Assumes back up responsibility for team member/line manager for service provider management and import export operations as required Requirements Education Degree / Diploma (with relevant work experience) in Logistics/Supply Chain/Business Management or related field Working Experience Minimum 3 years of experience in handling Import/Export logistics with customs and trade compliance background, liaison with logistics service providers. Experience in SAP and chemical industry preferred Technical & Professional Knowledge Efficient in MS Office applications such as Excel, PowerPoint and Word Basic understanding on Import & Export regulations. Knowledge in SAP system (TM/ SD/ MM Module) is preferred. Experience on using Cobalt or S4 Hana. Proactive team members who take ownership and show persistence to achieve results and exceed expectations. Ability to work independently on problem solving and identifying solutions. Good interpersonal skills and a team player Good command of written and spoken English is necessity. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. name: Breeding Agronomist
description: Breeding Agronomist Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Breeding Agronomist LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now About BASF At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees contribute to the BASF Group’s success worldwide. Our business comprises the Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions segments. We want to contribute to a better world with enhanced quality of life for everyone. That is why we have firmly anchored the three pillars of sustainability – the economy, environment and society – into our corporate purpose, our strategy, our targets and our operations. They are the core of what we do, a driver for growth and an element of our risk management. We believe that chemistry has a critical role to play in achieving greater sustainability. We are determined to move forward in this respect. Our employees are at the forefront of this transformation, across every role and function in the organization. Join us today in shaping a sustainable future together. About The Role At BASF we are entering an exciting time within our Agriculture business with an existing portfolio of world class products and a pipeline of new products that will bring significant benefits to the agriculture industry. As a Breeding Agronomist, you will enjoy a varied role conducting field trials and plant breeding experiments. You'll be responsible for planning and executing field trials, inclusive of trial site management, data collection and communication with our colleagues. How You Will Do It Establish and maintain field trials to produce data and seed, maintain all breeding records, update breeding database with trial and seed production results. Responsible in all aspects of the field program; locating and contracting land, land preparation and all in-season agronomy, following trial protocol and implementing treatments, harvest of trial and shipping of seed. Collaborate with breeders in the conduct of experiments and seed productions in growth chambers at Longerenong. Manage the supply of all key inputs (land, pollinators, contractors, materials) to ensure an efficient and reliable supply is maintained. Be field-based and hands on to ensure operations & activities are on time and to budget. Ensure all operations satisfy safety, regulatory, stewardship and compliance requirements. What We Look For To be successful in this role, you’ll have previous experience in an agronomy-based role and an understanding or interest in plant breeding or agricultural research.. You’ll have a strong focus on detail and enjoy a role with a balance of planning and hands-on operations. You will: Have a degree in Agricultural Science or similar discipline Have 2-10 years of experience conducting field trials Have experience in operating trial equipment and/or farm machinery. Have sound knowledge of canola agronomy. Understanding of hybrid seed production, particularly in relation to canola. Have knowledge of procedures used in agricultural field trials and plant breeding. Show natural leadership in ensuring that people follow guidelines and plan effectively. Demonstrate the ability to collaborate with a diverse range of people. Display strong communication and interpersonal skills. Have a valid driver’s license. A MR or HR license would be beneficial. What We Can Offer You At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a wide range of elements to suit each one of our global team. By creating chemistry with us, you can look forward to: A diverse and inclusive environment, with an open and fair internal promotion process A competitive parental leave policy, regardless of gender, and a supportive return to work plan Give back to your community with our corporate volunteering scheme 4 weeks annual leave, with the offer to purchase additional leave throughout the year Opportunities to apply for international assignments – the perks of a global business! Connect to Create Chemistry If you’d like to join a diverse global business and create chemistry for a sustainable future, we want to hear from you! Click ‘apply’ below. BASF is proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer for all women. If you’d like to know more information about how we are supporting our employees, you can view our policies and benefits here . Offers of employment will be subject to background checks including references, police checks and may include a pre-employment medical. Beware, Job Applicants. At BASF, we are committed to providing a safe and transparent recruitment process. Please be aware of job scams and fraudulent job postings. Only apply through the official BASF Career website to ensure your application is received by us. Be cautious of unsolicited job offers or communications that do not come from official BASF email addresses. Verify any doubtful communications through our HR department. Never provide personal information or make payment as part of the application process. #LI-Jobs Apply now Our people are at the heart of everything, dedicated to delivering value for our customers every day. By focusing on enabling our people today and in the future, we will ensure we have the talent and skills needed to succeed. United by the love of farming, we innovate, collaborate, and strive for the best solutions together, creating connections and driving success as one team. This is how we make an impact. For the love of farming, the biggest job on Earth. Longerenong, AUS BASF Australia Ltd. Permanent 134838 RnD Australia On-site Credits Data protection Contact Follow us Opens in a new tab. description:

We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjectives of the Position The Customs Data Manager supports & ensures Global consistency of Customs Data provided by Customs Authorities, 3rd party companies and BASF internal data sources. He/She manages and supervises the mapping and validation of the data and transfers them into the BASF Customs dashboards and other Data apps or applications. He/She is the key contact for all related request from the Corporate Centers and Operating divisions for key Customs and Duty figures, detailed analysis and deep dive consultancy on the duty impact, rates and new regulations. In addition, observation of political and economic changes and the translation of them into end-2-end Customer processes​ is expected. This incorporates the ability for deep dive data analytics and data presentation in standardized visual formats such as Customs Dashboard as well as the ownership of the Customs Data globally. He/She is also closely cooperating with Customs Duty Optimization role and interacts with Business units to optimize duty spend and focusing on reduction of duty liability. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Data Management Owner of the Customs Dashboards, ensuring Authorization concepts Owner of the Tariff simulation App and manages development Increase transparency on new laws, regulations and customs agreements​ and build in the changes into the Dashboard & App Initiates and defines regional binding GB Guidelines to ensure adherence to consistent Customs data Define, develop and monitor global Customs KPI and upload it into the GB Scorecard Proactively engages Customs Communities of practices to ensure globally defined processes Closely align activities with Process Design and AI Team to implement necessary changes in the process landscape Deep dive into and analyses of BASF internal & external customs data and presentation in a visual standardized format in close collaboration with Supply Chain Excellence team Provide Training to Customs Community Deep dive into and analyses of BASF internal & external customs data Interface with Supply Chain Excellence team on the dashboard data engineering . Data Analysis & Reporting Analyze customs data to identify trends, risks, and opportunities for improvement. Prepare and present compliance reports and dashboards to regional and global leadership. Monitor regulatory developments and assess their impact on business operations. Collaboration & Stakeholder Engagement Collaborate with customs communities, business units and supply chain functions. Act as the primary contact for customs communities, regional business units and supply chain on Customs Dashboard topics Job Requirements Education Bachelor’s degree in International Trade, Law, Supply Chain, Business Analytics or a related field. Working Experience 5+ years of experience in customs compliance, trade compliance, supply chain or customs operations, with regional or global exposure. Strong understanding of customs regulations, broker management, and internal control systems. Excellent analytical, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Technical & Professional Knowledge Good Customs and Foreign Trade knowledge and regulations Good Supply Chain Operations knowledge Strong communication and argumentation skills Strong analytics thinking Strong teamwork competence Structured work organization Experience in project management is desirable Good knowledge of the SAP master data systems and SAGP GTS Proficiency in customs software, ERP systems (e.g., SAP), and Microsoft Office Suite. The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Main responsibilities Perform Sales and Delivery Order Management tasks and responsible for CS front-end activities which includes, but not limited to, interfacing with Customers to build service relationships, collaborating with sales team to address business/ customer needs, coordinating with Logistics and / or 3rd party Logistics service providers on delivery arrangements to customers and enhancing customer proximity to support the business growth. Collaborate closely with the back-office services (APTC) for Customer Order Management to ensure timely fulfillment of orders. Contribute to development and communication related to order management in customer services e.g. product availability, order status and delivery tracking (Shipment & delivery monitoring including follow up with shipping documents). Support Credit Management on billing and Accounts Receivable mafollow-upsuch as monthly closing and billing correction, handling invoice discrepancies, monitoring customer payment schedule and supporting on following-up on overdue payment with customers or Sales persons if any, processing credit/debit notes and rebates, etc. Manage customer complaints via Non-conformance Management (NCM) system (raise NCMs, monitoring investigation and closure) and actively communicate the status, investigation results, corrective actions to customers and ensure on time closure for non-product quality related complaints. Transform customer perception by making service faster, simpler and more cost-efficient. Provide order status report and monitor customers’ order placement against sales forecast and communicate with Salespersons if orders are not within forecasts. Support the implementation of business rules related to customer order management process. Additional tasks within Customer Service team – provide efficient back-up and take up additional responsibilities upon assignment by Team Lead. Qualifications Bachelor's degree, or master's degree in business, marketing, or supply chain management is preferred. Minimum 3 years customer service, business, supply chain experience with demonstrated people skills. Expertise in order to cash processes in SAP environment essential. Good communication in Thai and English. Good computer skill. Work location Emporium Office, Bangkok BASF has been present in Thailand for more than 50 years. The company offers a wide range of products and solutions to support almost all the key industries in the country, including Agricultural Solutions, Intermediates and Process Catalysts, Monomers, Petrochemicals, Coatings, Dispersions and Resins, Performance Chemicals, Care Chemicals, Nutrition and Health, and Performance Materials. BASF in Thailand was established in 1966 and maintains its head office in Bangkok. It operates three production sites in Samutprakarn, Chonburi and Rayong, an Automotive Coatings Technical Competence Center, and a Performance Materials Technical Development Center in Samutprakarn. In 2024, BASF posted sales of €531 million to customers in Thailand and had 623 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/th . Objectives of the Position This position aims to provide end-to-end delivery services by efficiently handling customer service and logistics management to maintain business continuity, enhance service level and safety continuity in business. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Manage end-to-end daily order process and operation By collaborating with multiple functions (internal and external) including but not limited to customers, sales, GB, suppliers, finance, controlling. Escalate critical NCMs when needed for appropriate resolution. Technical Skills: Strong SAP competency is required. Personal Attributes: Thrives in a dynamic environment, maintains a positive and solutions‑oriented mindset, adapts well to change, collaborates effectively across teams, and consistently demonstrates strong professionalism and work ethic. name: Manufacturing Manager,ED,Mie-Rokuromi Plant 1
description:
Manufacturing Manager,ED,Mie-Rokuromi Plant 1 Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Manufacturing Manager,ED,Mie-Rokuromi Plant 1 LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Objectives of the Position This role is responsible for designing and installing optimal equipment in collaboration with the Engineering & Maintenance (E&M) team to support efficient and safe plant operations. It also involves training plant personnel to perform self-maintenance of equipment. Under the guidance of the Site Manager and in accordance with company policies, the role oversees production, quality, shipping, and receiving activities to ensure monthly sales targets are met with high quality and cost efficiency. Additionally, the role manages both existing and new biological wastewater treatment facilities, working closely with external partners and authorities to ensure environmental compliance. Main Responsibilities 1. Deputy Site Manager Duties Acts as the second deputy site manager when both the Site Manager and QA/Technical Manager are absent (excluding decisions on management reviews, long-term investments, HR matters, and budgeting). 2. Safety & Environmental Management Implements safety and environmental policies based on corporate and legal requirements. Conducts first aid training for all site employees and monitors safety attitudes. Serves as an Emergency Response Team member, leading on-site actions during emergencies and reporting to the central ER team. Leads EHS-related activities from a technical perspective as part of the Responsible Care (RC) committee. Identifies and improves unsafe working conditions in collaboration with the production team. 3. Production Management Develops production plans based on sales forecasts and R&D requirements. Coordinates procurement of raw materials and packaging. Revises production schedules in response to equipment issues. Participates in management meetings and collaborates with other departments to ensure smooth operations. Evaluates production performance, cost, and delivery timelines. Assesses feasibility of new product manufacturing and recommends necessary changes. Leads weekly production meetings and conducts regular site inspections. Oversees purchasing, inventory control, and interplant material coordination. 4. Waste Management Operates and supervises wastewater treatment facilities, ensuring water quality standards are met. Communicates with external partners and authorities regarding environmental compliance. Manages solid waste handling and audits waste disposal companies. 5. Equipment Design & Installation Plans and executes installation, relocation, and upgrades of machinery using new technologies. Designs, estimates costs, and installs electrical and instrumentation equipment. Manages individual investment projects from design to commissioning. Collaborates with local and regional technical teams to ensure equipment suitability. 6. Maintenance & Planning Develops maintenance schedules and spare parts inventory based on production needs. Participates in technical and process improvement planning. 7. Team & People Management Plans team structure, headcount, and budget. Coaches and evaluates team members, fostering skill development and succession planning. Reviews team performance and identifies areas for improvement. Implements strategies to enhance productivity, quality, and efficiency. Job Requirements University degree or equivalent Minimum 10 years of experience in plant operations or related fields Over 3 years of experience in people and team management Business-level English proficiency Native-level Japanese proficiency 製造部門マネージャー（部下あり） 勤務地：六呂見工場　三重県四日市市六呂見６５３−２ 主なミッション 製品製造プロセスおよび安全管理 チームメンバーへの助言・指導・育成。 主な責任領域と活動内容（Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities） 安全・衛生（Safety and Housekeeping）： 法令および社内方針に基づき、安全・環境保護施策を実施。工場長と連携し、作業環境の安全性を確保。 従業員の態度を常に観察し、応急処置訓練を全サイトで実施。 緊急対応メンバーとして、現場での対応を指揮し、工場長へ報告。 技術的観点からEHS関連活動をリード。 安全・衛生の改善活動を生産チームと連携して実施。 生産管理（General Production）： 売上予測やR&Dからの要求をもとに生産計画を立案。原材料・包装材・製品の調達を調整し、設備故障時には計画を修正。 生産戦略・設備投資計画を策定し、週次会議を主催。現場巡回により異常の早期発見と対策を実施。 購買・在庫管理手順を設定し、資材の調達契約を調整。 廃棄物管理（Waste Management）： 排水処理施設の運用・監督。水質管理のため当局と連携し、コスト管理を実施。 固形廃棄物の適切な処理を監督し、廃棄物業者の監査を実施。 設備設計・導入（Design and Installation）： 新技術を用いた設備の設置・移設・改良に関するプロジェクトに参加（単独投資プロジェクトの設計から試運転まで） 国内のエンジニアリング統括チームやグローバルの技術チームとの連携。 計画（Planning）： 生産計画に基づき、保全計画と予備部品の在庫レベルを策定。技術・工程改善計画の立案に参加。 人材・チーム管理（People and Team Management）： 組織構成・人員計画・予算を管理。チームの業務優先順位を設定し、教育・育成・後継者計画を実施。 作業記録を分析し、生産性・品質・コストを評価。継続的な改善を推進。 求められる資格・経験（Job Requirements） 学歴：大学卒業または同等の資格 経験：化学メーカーの製造工場での勤務経験10年以上、管理職経験を5年以上お持ちの方 必要な技術・専門知識： 計装および電気分野の知識・経験 英語力（グローバルMTGでの自チームの取り組みの紹介/日常的なメールやチャットでのやり取りなど） [受賞講演(3) ファクトリーマネジメント賞 BASFジャパン株式会社　六呂見事業所 【第13回】2025 GOOD FACTORY賞® 受賞企業講演会 - JMA活動レポート｜ JMAマネジメント Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. description:

To ensure timely payment of receivables from customers In addition, provide inputs and participate in the marketing, market planning and technical development of products and services. Specifications and BD: Establishing rapport with Specifiers - architects, consultants and designers in public and private organizations in particular with RDSO, EIL, HPCL, ONGC and know about all their upcoming projects. Focusing on Specifiers in chosen segments for specification selling in line with our Segment Strategy, to get our systems specified at the design stage of projects. Generation of sales potential (Volume) meeting quarterly and yearly targets through specification of our systems and through breaking competitors’ specifications in our favour. Assisting in organising technical seminars, etc to key Specifiers organisations. Main Tasks Sales: Stimulate and develop market intelligence to pursue market growth and deepen market penetration, e.g. attracting new customers (including distributors), applications, and innovations. Take responsibility for the overall sales process and coordination of interfaces to other departments/functions. Monitor & control local DSO and DIV Develop, implement, and monitor agreed sales strategies (including pricing implementation) To maintain strategic long term business relationship with customers To Provide timely Forecast accurately & make close co ordination with SCM to manage the RM and FG stock effectively. Specifications: No. of specifications achieved Vs objective in targeted key organisations and their potential sales value. Knowledge of construction related problems and system solutions. No. of new consultants / specifying organisations with whom rapport has been developed. Acquired level of market intelligence. No. of internal and external communications Vs Objective Additional: Key account Manager for global & regional key accounts and act as point of contact for Oil and Gas industry . General: Promote BASF at various trade platforms, customers by attending exhibitions, Technical days, seminars etc. Consistently and continuously exhibit safe behaviour at driving, project sites, offices and for self and others. Minimum Education and qualification required for the Position Education: B Tech. Working Experience: 10 years of experience Technical & Professional Knowledge: 1. Core skills: Conflict  Management Broad Thinking & taking Corrective actions proactively Work Management Should be team player Good Knowledge on cost analysis 2. name: Chemist
description:
Chemist Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Chemist LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Main responsibilities Provide lab support to Care Chemicals business in ASEAN to promote BASF raw materials (Surfactants, Chelates, Polymers etc.) in Agrochemical markets. Executing various types of lab activities such as new technologies evaluations, promoting existing Agrochemical portfolio of ingredients in relevant markets, development of new experimental methods. It requires working on customer problems and broader market requirements. Support business development from a technical point of view: developing lab solutions based on customers feedback and communicate to customers under guidance of supervisor. Develop new applications based on existing portfolio under guidance of supervisor. Promotion of new R&D products and preparation of promotion materials thereof. Analyze, conclude and initiate lab work under guidance of supervisor. Maintain the workplace and working environment that support work safety and efficient production activities according to plant safety regulations as well as Company EHS guideline, RC Requirements, Quality system ISO 9001, EFfCI GMP, Halal, RSPO and Energy management. Key interfaces: sub-regional sales, regional technical teams, customers. Qualifications Bachelor’s or master’s degree in chemistry/industrial & applied chemistry/physical chemistry or related fields. Minimum 2 years’ experience in laboratory or relevant working experience. Ability to quickly understand customers and translate into lab work, team working, effective communication and collaboration skills under the multicultural environments. Know- how of Agrochemical formulations, hands on experience on various test methods and lab equipment used in preparation of emulsions/ solutions/ dispersions for Agrochemical applications is a PLUS. Good communication skills for English. Work location Bangpakong, Chonburi BASF has been present in Thailand for more than 50 years. The company offers a wide range of products and solutions to support almost all the key industries in the country, including Agricultural Solutions, Intermediates and Process Catalysts, Monomers, Petrochemicals, Coatings, Dispersions and Resins, Performance Chemicals, Care Chemicals, Nutrition and Health, and Performance Materials. BASF in Thailand was established in 1966 and maintains its head office in Bangkok. It operates three production sites in Samutprakarn, Chonburi and Rayong, an Automotive Coatings Technical Competence Center, and a Performance Materials Technical Development Center in Samutprakarn. In 2023, BASF posted sales of €570 million to customers in Thailand and had 625 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/th . name: Order Management (Executive)
description:
Order Management (Executive) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Order Management (Executive) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Objectives of the Position The Executive, Order Management, will execute the customer order management processes with a consistent high level of service across ASEAN, Australia and New Zealand (ANZ), Taiwan for the Customer Service which is still with Global Business. Main Tasks Operations and Performance Execute the Customer Order Management processes set up in KL Hub with the different models & interfaces Processing of sales and purchase orders from various customers in a timely manner Service contact for assigned countries – monitor order status and provide update information to the countries and customers Bill of Lading draft checking (for Letter of Credit orders, 3rd party supplier orders and key customer orders) Manage Insurance Certificate (if necessary) Follow up Bill of Lading receipt status based on Estimate Time of Delivery Collection of Original Bill of Lading and other documents from goods suppliers Provide full and accurate required documents to customers Upload / maintain documents in SAP for audit trail Support documents for internal and external audit purpose Register all and follow-up with all non-conformances in the NCM system to ensure a quick and efficient customer response Participate proactively in new workflow for on time & accurate documents Ensure that delivery capability and reliability of all orders Ensure that a minimum internal cycle time of order processing is achieved by improving the process wherever possible Verifying pricing with Marketing and execute & monitor all guidelines implemented Troubleshooting and problem solving Process debit and credit notes wherever applicable (Business Unit) Requirements Education Bachelor degree or High Diploma preferably in Business or Supply Chain Management Working Experience 2-4 years relevant experience in supply chain management, including hands-on experience in order management and shipping document handling Technical & Professional Knowledge Supply Chain Management / process knowledge, SAP (Sales and Distributions module) experience, operational order management including shipping documentation, foreign documentary trade, good communication skills. BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. Buyer - Tokyo LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Regional Category Buyer （Assistant Manager but non-manager Role ） ■ポジション概要 本ポジションは、日本市場向けパフォーマンスケミカル（スチレン、グリコール類など）および原材料およびエネルギー（天然ガス・工業用ガス・電力・再生可能エネルギー等）の調達戦略を担い、競争力ある調達と安定供給の実現に貢献いただく重要な役割です。サプライヤーとの戦略的交渉・契約管理・関係構築を通じてコスト最適化を推進しながら、日本市場における市場の分析（政策、料金体系、インセンティブ、主要プレイヤー、投資動向等）を行い、持続可能かつ効率的な調達体制の構築をリードいただきます。 化学業界特有の市場変動・供給リスク・品質要求に対応しながら、事業価値を最大化する戦略的購買ポジション です。 iBP、製造拠点、グローバル調達組織、サプライヤーと密に連携し、 価格交渉に留まらず、安定供給・リスクマネジメント・プロセス改善まで幅広く関与 していただきます。 このポジションの魅力 ・化学品購買の専門性を活かせる／高められる ・グローバル企業における戦略的調達ポジションで環境エネルギー・サステナビリティ領域に関わる最前線の業務 ・上司が海外、同僚も全員が海外。日本オフィスに籍は置くものの、インターナショナルな環境での勤務が可能 ・SAP、Coupa 等の先進的調達プラットフォームを活用 職務の目的（Objectives） サプライヤーとの交渉を通じ、競争力のある価格および最適な購買条件を実現する 契約条件の交渉、契約締結後の実行・モニタリングを担う iBP の原材料購買要件（品質・数量・供給安定性）に対し、最適なソリューションを提供する 生産拠点・事業部門・サプライヤーと連携し、安定かつ持続可能な供給体制を構築する サプライヤーマネジメント、リスクマネジメント、シングルソース管理を推進する 日本向けパフォーマンスケミカルの購買および市場・サプライヤー分析を実施する 主な職務内容（Key Responsibilities） 1. 調達・契約交渉 担当原材料およびエネルギー関連製品における最適な購買条件の設定・価格交渉 サプライヤーとの契約条件交渉、契約締結および履行管理 日本におけるエネルギー・ユーティリティ契約の交渉・導入 2. サプライヤーマネジメント サプライヤーとの関係構築・維持・改善 サプライヤー評価およびパフォーマンス管理 リスク管理およびシングルソースに対する対応策の実行 3. 市場分析・戦略提案 日本の化学・エネルギー市場の分析およびサプライヤー分析 エネルギー市場（政策、料金制度、投資動向、主要プレイヤー）の調査 コスト効率およびサステナビリティを考慮した供給モデルの提案 4. 社内連携・ソリューション提供 生産拠点・事業部門・関連部門と連携し、安定供給の確保 内部ビジネスパートナーの要件（仕様、物流、需要動向）の把握と最適提案 緊急対応時のサポートおよびカテゴリーチームとの連携 5. カテゴリー戦略およびプロジェクト推進 チームリードおよびリージョナルカテゴリーマネージャーと連携し、調達戦略の立案・検討・実行 「内製 vs 外購」の検討やサプライチェーン最適化の検討への参画 6. オペレーション・プロセス管理 SAPにおける購買申請の承認プロセス管理 監査対応・マスターデータ管理のためのサプライヤー情報収集 品質不良（NCM）発生時のサプライヤー交渉および改善推進 応募資格（Requirements） 必須条件 化学業界における購買・営業部門での実務経験（目安：3年以上） 日本語での業務遂行能力および高い英語力（読み書き・会話） 歓迎条件 化学品（原料・中間体・スペシャリティケミカル）やエネルギーの購買または販売経験 SAP、Coupa 等の購買システム使用経験 求める人物像 社内外ステークホルダーに影響を与えられる交渉力・調整力 問題解決力とビジネス価値創出への意識 自律的に考え、改善提案ができるマインドセット Objectives of the Position · Ensure competitive and reliable procurement of raw materials (chemicals and, where applicable, energy/utilities -defined here as Natural Gas, Industrial Gas, Electricity and Renewable Energy) for BASF Japan through effective negotiation, contract management, and supplier strategy. Perform Energy and Utility market study in Japan, including policy development, tariff systems, incentive schemes, market trading, major equipment manufacturers, developers, utilities, investment fund and project pipelines. · Negotiate with supplier for the most competitive price and/or purchase condition within given boundary · Negotiate terms and conditions for contract, contract implementation & monitoring · Manage supplier relationships and support the needs of internal business partners. · Routinely engage with BASF production sites, business units and suppliers to ensure reliable supply and provide effective solution to iBP meeting their requirements · Execute Supplier Management · Execute Risk Management and Single Source Management · Understand Japanese Chemical Market and make supplier and market analysis of the relevant products Minimum Education and Qualification required for the Position Bachelor’s degree major in Chemistry, Chemical Engineering or Economics, Business Management +2 years working experience in procurement Preferred to have experience in chemical purchase/sales management Native or fluent Japanese speaker and excellent level of verbal & written English Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. description:
