We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees contribute to the BASF Group’s success worldwide. Our business comprises the Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions segments. We want to contribute to a better world with enhanced quality of life for everyone. That is why we have firmly anchored the three pillars of sustainability – the economy, environment and society – into our corporate purpose, our strategy, our targets and our operations. They are the core of what we do, a driver for growth and an element of our risk management. We believe that chemistry has a critical role to play in achieving greater sustainability. We are determined to move forward in this respect. Our employees are at the forefront of this transformation, across every role and function in the organization. Join us today in shaping a sustainable future together. About The Role This role is within the Performance Management and Strategic Controlling Team of the Dispersions & Resins Division. Reporting to the team head, the intern will support monthly reports, develop and maintain financial dashboards with Power BI, analyze financial trends, support compliance process and participate in ad hoc projects. How You Will Do It Support the regular management reporting to senior management of our global operating division Develop and maintain financial dashboards using Power BI to transform financial data into valuable insights for business steering Assist in driving digitalization initiatives for reporting and analysis tools Conduct financial trend and variance analysis to identify opportunities for growth and efficiency Assist with ad hoc assignments or projects to support Controlling and Strategic initiatives Participate in global meetings to collaborate with leaders and teams from different regions and functions What We Look For Commitment to a 6-month full-time internship from mid-July 2026 Students of Bachelor’s Degree or Master’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Business Analytics, or related disciplines Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office tools, including Excel, PowerPoint and Power BI Experience in data analysis or business controlling is a plus Excellent oral and written English communication skills Demonstrates accountability, critical thinking, and strong problem-solving skills A self-starter who can work independently and manage projects with minimal supervision For more information about BASF Internship Program, please visit Your internship at BASF: the foundation for your career BASF has a rich history in Hong Kong, dating back to the 1880s when it began selling textile dyes in the region. Over the years, the company has grown and expanded its presence, establishing its East Asia Regional Headquarters in Hong Kong in 1995. As a multinational company with a long-standing presence in Hong Kong, BASF is committed to the city's continued success as an internationally competitive business and financial center. By the end of 2025, BASF East Asia Regional Headquarters Limited had 240 employees. Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a

description:
Product Safety, Senior Specialist (Japanese Speaker) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Product Safety, Senior Specialist (Japanese Speaker) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now O bjectives of the Position Deliver expert regulatory and product stewardship support to BASF group companies, ensuring full compliance with corporate policies, guidelines, and global standards. Collaborate effectively with cross-functional stakeholders including supply chain, customer service, and technical teams to drive the implementation of compliance policies and BASF minimum requirements. Offer expert advice and practical solutions through thorough impact assessment to support sound decisions and effective risk management. Continuous improvement through applicable methodologies and digitalization to drive operation excellence. Main Areas of Responsibilities Conduct comprehensive hazard assessments using (e.g., GHS, CLP, REACH, equivalent regional/local regulations) standards utilizing product composition, test data, and toxicological/ecotoxicological information. Maintain hazard communication documentation: Safety Data Sheets (SDS), labels, hazard classification, ensuring consistency and compliance with relevant classification & labelling regulations Apply advanced regulatory expertise across multiple jurisdictions to deliver expert judgments on chemical classification, labelling, and compliance requirements. Drive critical decisions that safeguard product safety and ensure full regulatory adherence. Proactively monitor, interpret, and implement regulatory changes with precision. Lead comprehensive impact assessments on products, processes, and business operations. Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to implement compliance strategies, address regulatory requirements, and support advocacy activities. Provide regulatory guidance, training, and support to internal stakeholders, ensure awareness and understanding of regulatory obligations, chemical hazards and compliance procedures. Job Requirements University degree, Master’s degree in scientific area, preferably in Chemistry, or related Science discipline At least 3-4 years of relevant experience in Chemical Regulation, Product Regulatory Affairs, or related fields focused on Product Safety and Stewardship activities. Technical & Professional Knowledge Sound knowledge and experience in Asia Pacific countries regulatory requirements (REACH, GHS/CLP, Global & Regional Chemical Regulations), Toxicology & Ecotoxicology, Chemical Safety & SDS Authoring and Product Safety/Stewardship framework. Proficiency in MS Office is preferred and experience in SAP application is an advantage. Fluent in written and spoken English and Japanese. Experience in technology or digital related skills or tools is an advantage, e.g. Robotic Process Automation, Power BI, etc. Excellent analytical, organizational, communication and interpersonal skills. Demonstrate critical thinking and effective problem-solving skills as necessary Details oriented, proactive and able to work independently About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. BASF posted sales to customers in Malaysia of approximately €340 million in 2024 and had 2,579 employees as of the end of the year. description:
Technical Sales Representative - Melbourne Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Technical Sales Representative - Melbourne LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now About BASF At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees contribute to the BASF Group’s success worldwide. Our business comprises the Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, Surface Technologies, Nutrition & Care and Agricultural Solutions segments. We want to contribute to a better world with enhanced quality of life for everyone. That is why we have firmly anchored the three pillars of sustainability – the economy, environment and society – into our corporate purpose, our strategy, our targets and our operations. They are the core of what we do, a driver for growth and an element of our risk management. We believe that chemistry has a critical role to play in achieving greater sustainability. We are determined to move forward in this respect. Our employees are at the forefront of this transformation, across every role and function in the organization. Join us today in shaping a sustainable future together. About The Role As a Technical Sales Representative, you will implement technical, colour and digital customer solutions, support sales acquisition by demonstration and validation of BASF solutions, development of distribution technical teams and supporting new product, digital & colour service launch within the described territory. How You Will Do It Maintain required customer and distributor visit cycles, meeting objectives, and action plans based on business rules and sales priorities. Provide day‑to‑day technical, colour, and training support to customers and distributors within the assigned territory. Conduct new customer acquisition demos, including digital and colour solutions, showcasing BASF’s value propositions versus competitors. Support BASF and distributor sales teams in presenting technical demo outcomes to target customers. Implement digital and colour solutions (including Refinity) across all assigned customers. Identify painter skill gaps, deliver onsite training, resolve customer complaints promptly, and conduct technical vehicle inspections with reporting. Execute system conversions, customer trainings, and technology transfers while identifying improvement opportunities and demonstrating BASF advantages. Update Customer Skills Assessment and Sales Force databases regularly as required. Maintain required technical competencies, complete all training (including e‑learning), and collaborate with Technical Process Specialists to maximize customer productivity, efficiency, and repair quality. What We Look For Either hold a tertiary qualification in Automotive Refinish or equivalent spray painter qualification. Certificate IV in Workplace Assessment and Training is preferred. At least 6 years of refinish industry working experience. Experienced in coaching and development, delivering and communicating information to a diverse audience. Experience in using modern auto refinish materials preferred but not essential Proficient in Microsoft Office, including Excel, Word, and PowerPoint. name: Executive, Import Export Malaysia
description:
Executive, Import Export Malaysia Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Executive, Import Export Malaysia LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position: Ensure effective execution of inbound and outbound transportation (Import & Export) including documentation and system (SAP TM) processes Maintain accurate reporting, cost control, and compliance with company and regulatory requirements Drive timely issue resolution, escalation, and continuous improvement across logistics operations Support monitoring of service provider performance against defined KPIs Main Tasks: 1. Operations Management Manage end-to-end Import & Export shipment processes (Air, LCL, FCL, ISO, tanker, parcel tanker) Coordinate shipment arrangements with LLP, LSP, warehouse, PPC, and stakeholders Prepare and validate shipping documentation and ensure proper system updates (SAP TM) Handle MDB block release and shipment readiness validation Troubleshoot system or operational issues impacting shipment execution 2. Reporting & Analytics Prepare and manage operational reports including: LLP Rework Report LCL/Air KPI Report TCO Report Demurrage & Detention Report Freight Cost Clean-up (weekly & monthly) FCV bi-weekly updates Validate data with LLP/LSP and ensure submission within defined timelines Analyze deviations and drive corrective actions 3. Cost Control & Billing Verify freight invoices and resolve discrepancies with FCV and LSP Manage trailer detention and demurrage/detention validation and approval Perform freight cost clean-up and ensure closure of open items Support PR issuance based on PO balance review and operational requirements 4. Claim & Issue Management Manage Claim Billing and Non-Conformance Management (NCM) Act as coordinator for insurance claims involving Legal, warehouse, and service providers Investigate root causes and implement corrective actions for operational issues Maintain issue logs and ensure closure of all reported cases 5. Spot & Rate Management Manage spot rate requests (FCL, LCL, Air, Flexi, ISO) when not covered under contracts Ensure proper approval prior to shipment execution Maintain spot rates and supporting documentation in systems and SharePoint 6. Compliance & Audit Perform health checks on operations and documentation compliance Ensure all supporting documents are properly archived (SharePoint / TM / systems) Support internal and external audit requirements Ensure compliance with VGM (SOLAS) and other regulatory requirements 7. Knowledge & Process Management Develop and maintain Work Instructions (WI), guidelines, and job aids Upload and maintain documents in KNOWIT and SharePoint Support process improvements and system enhancements 8. Stakeholder & Service Provider Management Liaise with LLP, LSP, procurement, warehouse, PPC, finance (FCV/P2P), and Hub teams Monitor service provider performance against KPIs Support performance reviews and continuous improvement initiatives Handle enquiries related to shipment arrangements and logistics rates 9. System & Data Management Maintain SAP TM master data including route, partner (Z5), and cost elements Support troubleshooting on shipment cost, FOB value, and invoice generation issues Ensure data accuracy and system compliance Minimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position: • Education: Degree or above in international trade, logistics, supply chain or business management. Advanced diploma to be considered for candidates with more years of experience in customs and international trade. • Working Experience: 1–3 year’s experience in Import/Export operations, logistics coordination, and documentation Exposure to working with logistics service providers (LLP/LSP) Experience in SAP TM / MM and chemical industry is an advantage • Technical & Professional Knowledge: Proficient in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, Word) Knowledge of SAP TM processes and transportation management systems Strong analytical and problem-solving skills Ability to manage multiple stakeholders and operational priorities Good communication skills (written & spoken English) High level of ownership, accountability, and attention to detail The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Customer plans, sales targets, key performance indicators, and crop marketing plan to contribute to crop annual budget Represent the company and introduce value-based proposals according to the needs of the key customers while building long-term partnerships and generating sustainable sales. Manage connections customers have with retailers in the region Provide market, price, product, grower, and consumer insight to contribute to the formulation of the vision and strategy plans for the crop Collaborate and network amongst several crop teams to understand crop strategy and implement marketing plans and business plans Collaborate with internal (global) stakeholders to evaluate and promote varietal performance and new introductions. Execute go to market strategies and policies to the coming years in the scope of commercial excellence in the region for the cucumber, tomato and capsicum crops. What We Look For Well-rounded knowledge of vegetable seed business Possess a Bachelor’s degree with 5–8 years of sales experience; experience in marketing and/or project management is strongly preferred Advanced consultative selling skills, including negotiation and sales closing Strong entrepreneurial mindset, with the ability to influence stakeholders across diverse backgrounds and functions Strong agronomic knowledge with project management skills Strong English communication skills are required Excellent communication skills, sound judgment, and strong organisational abilities Ability to thrive in a fast-paced environment and manage multiple competing priorities effectively Marketing and product management skills to position and price varieties effectively within the marketplace Ability to collaborate with global colleagues on portfolio strategy and development Connect to Create Chemistry If you’d like to join a diverse global business and create chemistry for a sustainable future, we want to hear from you! Click ‘apply’ below. BASF is proud to be a WORK180 Endorsed Employer for all women. If you’d like to know more information about how we are supporting our employees, you can view our policies and benefits here . Offers of employment will be subject to background checks including references, police checks and may include a pre-employment medical. Beware, Job Applicants. At BASF, we are committed to providing a safe and transparent recruitment process. Please be aware of job scams and fraudulent job postings. Only apply through the official BASF Career website to ensure your application is received by us. Be cautious of unsolicited job offers or communications that do not come from official BASF email addresses. Verify any doubtful communications through our HR department. Never provide personal information or make payment as part of the application process. #LI-Jobs Apply now Our people are at the heart of everything, dedicated to delivering value for our customers every day. By focusing on enabling our people today and in the future, we will ensure we have the talent and skills needed to succeed. United by the love of farming, we innovate, collaborate, and strive for the best solutions together, creating connections and driving success as one team. This is how we make an impact. For the love of farming, the biggest job on Earth. Melbourne, AUS BASF Australia Ltd. Permanent 138831 Others Australia Remote Credits Data protection Contact Follow us Opens in a new tab. name: Senior Officer, New Substance Notification
description:
Senior Officer, New Substance Notification Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Senior Officer, New Substance Notification LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now About The Role Our EC/ES Product Stewardship APAC unit plays a crucial role in ensuring compliance with national and international chemical legislation. As the first point of contact for Product Stewardship topics, we actively engage with stakeholders, monitor regulatory developments, and promote responsible chemical management throughout the lifecycle of our products. By sharing our knowledge and expertise, we aim to ensure that Product Stewardship is integrated into all aspects of our operations. How You Will Do It • You are together with the Chemical Regulations Manager the primary contact for ECs business units for Asian chemical regulations. In close cooperation with the EC global regulatory team, you stay up to date with the latest regulations and requirements. This helps us to ensure compliance with the legal regulations for EC operations (ECO, ECR, ECT) for finished products in APAC. • Conducting hazard assessments and risk evaluations of our products from a chemical and regulatory perspective is another important task for you. By strategically identifying emerging regulatory issues and opportunities, you assess product and marketing risks and proactively develop risk management measures and product strategies for assigned portfolio and business units. • In Asia several countries require registrations. You will support preparing and submitting New Substance Notifications and (Product) Registration applications in accordance with local and regional regulations for more than 10 countries. This will require collaboration with internal stakeholders, including R&D, Manufacturing, and Quality Assurance, to gather necessary data for submissions. • Monitoring of registered substance and being responsible for the Substance Volume Tracking for the Asian countries is another daily task including SAP maintenance. What We Look For • Completed scientific studies (B.Sc or M. Sc), preferably in chemistry. • Regulatory affairs experience, specifically in Asian Pacific chemical regulations. • Well-versed in IT applications, with at least 3 years working experience in SAP EHS system in product safety or chemical legislation area, coatings, fine chemical industrial relevant experience preferred. • High level of commitment and teamwork skills, flexibility, customer orientation, as well as independent and structured work approach. • Clear and authentic communication skills in English, with the ability to communicate effectively at all levels of the organization, translate regulatory/technical information clearly. • Entrepreneurial thinking, pragmatism, and hands-on mentality. 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用。 Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Sales & customer management • Plan and manage EMO (Oleo Chemicals & Alcohols) business including key accounts and direct accounts/dealers/distributors to meet sales target; ensure account strategies are exploited consistently including new opportunities. • Report all opportunities into Salesforce process at least one month in advance. • Maximize profitability by contributing to the full utilization of assets in Asia Pacific and not limited by local view • Enhance account relationships and meet sales targets for each assigned account and as per KPI’s via replacement or project activities including calling and visiting existing/prospective customers to fully understand and identify their needs to create a long-term customer supplier relationship. • Timely response to customer requests including product information, technical support via coordination with technical, customer services, logistics, Regional Pricing and Margin Management, regulatory affairs, etc as necessary to solve customer problem/NCM and to enhance customer service and satisfaction 2. Market intelligence • Collect data to establish a database to obtain, update, maintain and evaluate market trends, application of products/technology and service needs, etc in order to support identifying new business opportunities. Conduct initial analysis as necessary and requested. • Stay informed about industry trends, competitor activities, and regulatory changes to anticipate customer and market needs while providing strategic insights to the management. • Collet and analyze domestic brands’ financial viability,engagement& relationship potential, market position & competitive edge, strategic alignment, innovation & adaptability to select potential winners to collaborate. • Collect and monitor competitor activities, initially analyze data for market research to develop competitor grid and benchmarking on competitors’ positioning and the performance of their competitive products. • Analyze monthly sales/profit performance, proactively prevent business lost and initiate action for recovering • Provide timely reports to supervisor/ team members What We Look For • Education: Bachelor’s degree in Chemistry or related discipline • Working Experience: Proven successful performance with minimum 6 years’ working experience in sales & marketing • Other requirements: o Result oriented, able to identify and exploit business opportunities, commodity sales processes, EMO product line o Good communication and presentation skills o Selling and negotiation skills 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用。 Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. name: Automation Engineer (1 year contract)
description:
Automation Engineer (1 year contract) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Automation Engineer (1 year contract) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Do you want to work with passionate teams, providing excellent business and digital services to the Asia Pacific region? Do you want an open, supportive, and caring working environment? Do you view your development needs seriously as we do? If YES, then take your career to the #NextLevel with BASF ! Objectives of the Position BASF Shared Services Center Kuala Lumpur was established in 2005 to provide functional services in Finance, Controlling, Information Services, Human Resources, Environment, Health & Safety, Procurement, and Import-Export Order Management to BASF companies in the Asia-Pacific region. The center is part of BASF’s Global Business Services (GB) division, with three other sites in Berlin and Ludwigshafen in Germany and in Montevideo, Uruguay. GB’s Global Technology Unit (GTU) is looking for an Automation Engineer with a technical background in in automation technologies. The role works closely with GTU’s Global Business Enablement and solutions delivery teams to implement and support IT solutions for other units of the GB division globally. Additionally, it is envisioned for the role to be part of a global Community of Practice to further the awareness and adoption of Citizen Development technologies across the GTU and GB organization in Kuala Lumpur, Berlin and Montevideo. Main Tasks As an Automation Engineer in the Global Technology Unit team, your main tasks include: Designing, implementing and supporting automated workflows using UiPath RPA, Microsoft Power Automate and other related technologies Integrate automation solutions with enterprise systems and platforms (e.g. SAP, SharePoint, APIs, databases, and cloud services) by leveraging REST APIs and connectors to enable end-to-end process automation across multiple systems Collaborate with business stakeholders to assist in analyzing and mapping end-to-end business processes, identifying automation opportunities and optimization potential Define automation scope, requirements, and success criteria, ensuring alignment with business objectives Design scalable and maintainable automation solutions, including exception handling strategies and human-in-the-loop considerations where required Participating in testing activities, including any applicable unit testing, SIT, UAT, FAT etc. Creating and managing Continuous Integration and Continuous Deployment pipelines to automate the software application release process in-line with BASF best practices Monitoring and optimization of implemented solutions and the relevant infrastructure environments they run on, to minimize service disruption risks by analyzing metrics related to system/application health Supporting implemented solutions through troubleshooting and rectification Produce and maintain comprehensive technical documentation, including design documents, runbooks, and support materials Collaborate with technical teams across BASF’s technology landscape within the automation domain Ensure automation solutions adhere to BASF technology standards, security policies, and integration guidelines Apply governance practices for Power Platform and RPA environments, including access control, auditability, and compliance requirements Requirements Education: Bachelor Degree in Computer Science / Computer Engineering / Information Technology or business-technology related discipline. Working experience 3-5 years’ experience in automation development using UiPath and Microsoft Power Automate, including workflow design, reusable component development, exception handling, and application of RPA best practices (e.g. REFramework, orchestrated execution, scalable design patterns) Technical Skills (mandatory): Proven ability to integrate automation solutions with enterprise systems (e.g. SAP, SharePoint, databases, cloud services) using REST APIs, connectors, and data exchange mechanisms Solid understanding of data structures and processing (e.g. JSON, XML, SQL) to ensure reliable end-to-end automation Strong understanding of software development lifecycle (SDLC) and hands-on experience with CI/CD pipelines, version control, and automated deployment practices Ability to design and execute testing strategies (unit, integration, UAT) to ensure quality and stability of automation solutions Strong understanding of automation governance and security principles, including access control, environment management, and compliance with enterprise IT standards Ability to design and implement solutions aligned with Power Platform and enterprise governance models, ensuring auditability and controlled scalability Familiarity with Microsoft Azure ecosystem (e.g. Databricks, Azure SQL, Data Factory) and broader data services to support automation use cases Demonstrated strength in producing and maintaining clear, structured technical documentation (e.g. solution design, process documentation, runbooks) to support maintainability, auditability, and knowledge transfer Working experience in an MNCs setup, with cross-collaboration between multi-disciplinary teams virtually across multiple time zones Technical and Professional Knowledge (nice-to-have) Exposure to AI development using Microsoft Copilot Studio Soft-skill required: Digitally-savvy, innovative, highly passionate on new technology offerings, highly perceptive of what’s new in the technology space and its relevance to bring business value Strong analytical skills to steer engineering requirements and can drive high code standards Strong communication & team building skills, combined with an ability to manage and resolve conflict, change management skill in matrixed and virtual teams Enthusiastic, confident, entrepreneurial with sound business judgement within the given broad guidelines Ability to maintain customer-oriented focus even when dealing with technical subject matter Demonstrate a high discipline to complete tasks within project team timelines Objectives of the Position BASF Shared Services Center Kuala Lumpur was established in 2005 to provide functional services in Finance, Controlling, Information Services, Human Resources, Environment, Health & Safety, Procurement, and Import-Export Order Management to BASF companies in the Asia-Pacific region. The center is part of BASF’s Global Business Services (GB) division, with three other sites in Berlin and Ludwigshafen in Germany and in Montevideo, Uruguay. GB’s Global Technology Unit (GTU) is looking for a Full-Stack Developer with a technical background in building modern IT applications and solutions. The role works closely with GTU’s Global Business Enablement and solutions delivery teams to implement and support IT solutions for other units of the GB division globally. Additionally, it is envisioned for the role to be part of a global Community of Practice to further the awareness and adoption of Citizen Development technologies across the GTU and GB organization in Kuala Lumpur, Berlin and Montevideo. Main Tasks As a Full-Stack Developer in the Global Technology Unit team, your main tasks include: Build responsive, high‑quality front‑end user experiences using modern JS frameworks (component-based UI, state management, performance etc.) Design, develop, and maintain back‑end services and APIs in Python Get involved in database design, data storage integration, and performance tuning Ensure software quality by participating in testing activities (unit tests, SIT, UAT etc.), code reviews, and CI/CD-oriented engineering practices in-line with BASF standards Monitoring and optimization of implemented solutions and the relevant infrastructure environments they run on, to minimize service disruption risks by analyzing metrics related to system/application health Supporting implemented solutions through troubleshooting and rectification Produce and maintain comprehensive technical documentation, including design documents, runbooks, and support materials Collaborate with technical teams across BASF’s technology landscape within the automation domain Ensure solutions follow BASF technology standards, security policies, and integration guidelines Requirements Education: Bachelor Degree in Computer Science / Computer Engineering / Information Technology or business-technology related discipline. Working experience 3-5 years of experience in software development as a full-stack developer in a DevOps environment Technical Skills (mandatory): Strong proficiency in modern front-end development using JavaScript/TypeScript frameworks (preference for Vue.js) Back-end development experience using Python frameworks (preference for FastAPI) Experience with API development and lifecycle management tools and standards (e.g. Postman, OpenAPI/Swagger) Good understanding of database design and data management principles (PostgreSQL, MongoDB preferred) Hands-on experience working in a DevOps-oriented development environment using collaboration and delivery tools (e.g. Azure DevOps), including tasks like work item tracking, backlog management, code repositories, pull request workflows, and integration with CI/CD pipelines to support agile delivery and team collaboration. Ability to design and execute testing strategies (unit, integration, UAT etc.) to ensure quality and stability of developed solutions Strong understanding of governance and security principles, including access control, environment management, and compliance with enterprise IT standards Practical knowledge of application monitoring, troubleshooting, and performance optimization Demonstrated strength in producing and maintaining clear, structured technical documentation (e.g. solution design, process documentation, runbooks) to support maintainability, auditability, and knowledge transfer Working experience in an MNCs setup, with cross-collaboration between multi-disciplinary teams virtually across multiple time zones Technical and Professional Knowledge (nice-to-have) Experience in production-grade Python backend engineering Experience using Python for agent orchestration (Microsoft Agent Framework, LangChain / LangGraph) Soft-skill required: Digitally-savvy, innovative, highly passionate on new technology offerings, highly perceptive of what’s new in the technology space and its relevance to bring business value Strong analytical skills to steer engineering requirements and can drive high code standards Strong communication & team building skills, combined with an ability to manage and resolve conflict, change management skill in matrixed and virtual teams Enthusiastic, confident, entrepreneurial with sound business judgement within the given broad guidelines Ability to maintain customer-oriented focus even when dealing with technical subject matter Demonstrate a high discipline to complete tasks within project team timelines Appreciation on the importance of connecting tasks to the bigger picture BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre was established in 2005. How You Will Do It Assist Lab management for routine compounding lab activities and technical support Assist lab manager to manage compounding team members to achieve target KPI and team objectives. Monitoring the compounding process at the pilot plant during development or customer sample production. No customer samples will be produced when the process is not robust (e.g., pelletizing issues, die deposit issues, raw material handling or feeding issues, etc.). Deliver process guidelines and onsite support to setup process parameters when there is new process required. Conduct process DOE and provide process guideline for a challenging product to the PD and related function based on requests. Synthesis lab report and share to stakeholders for the key take outs from the internal/ external studies. Conduct and monitor compounding process scale up and plant support Assist lab manager to verify the compounding process window prior to PTO at the plant and sharing the findings with the MTC and operation team. Collaborating with PD, MTC, and operation teams to identify and implement improvement plans when production challenges are expected during the development process. Attending critical PTOs at the plant to support compounding process optimization. Manage Lab digitalization activities Assist lab manager to build up digitalized and automated lab to avoid human errors, to make digitalized data for further analysis, and to protect operators in terms of ergonomics. Drive for compouding process innovation Maintain an overview of the state of art of compounding process technology, equipment, and other accessories to build up internal competency to align customer and market needs. What We Look For Master Degree in Polymer Engineering, Science, Chemistry, Materials Science, Materials Engineering, Chemical Engineering, or equivalent. Candidates with relevant hands-on experience will also be considered. Minimum 3 years of work experience on engineering plastics compounding as an R&D process engineer, product developer, or plant process engineer. Good knowledge on scale up and industrialization. Good knowledge on statistical analysis based on lean 6 sigma tools and methodologies. Good communication skills in English. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Job Objectives : Industry Manager, ASEAN is responsible for leading and driving the overall sales strategy, business growth, and market expansion across the ASEAN sub-region for Dispersion Business Unit (Fiber Bonding & Adhesive, Paper & Carpet Dispersion Industry). This role oversee sub-regional sales operations, key account management, revenue growth, and customer relationship development while ensuring alignment with the company’s business objectives and profitability targets. The role requires strong leadership capabilities, strategic planning, and hands-on execution to achieve sustainable growth and strengthen the company’s market presence across ASEAN countries. Key Responsibilities Sales Strategy & Business Growth Develop and implement sub-region sales strategies to achieve revenue and profitability targets across ASEAN markets. Identify new business opportunities, market trends, and customer needs to expand the company’s market share. Drive sales growth through strategic partnerships, channel development, and key account acquisition. Monitor market conditions, competitor activities, and pricing strategies to maintain competitiveness. Sub-Region Sales Management Lead, coach, and manage country sales teams to ensure high performance and target achievement. Establish sales objectives, KPIs, and performance metrics for sub-region and country-level teams. Conduct regular sales reviews, forecasting, pipeline management, and business performance analysis. Ensure effective coordination and alignment among ASEAN sales teams. Key Account & Customer Relationship Management Build and maintain strong relationships with key customers and strategic partners. Support negotiations for major contracts and commercial agreements. Ensure high customer satisfaction and long-term business retention. Operational & Financial Management Take responsibility for financial performance to achieve agreed on targets regarding net sales, contribution margin and fixed costs Manage sub-regional sales budgets and ensure efficient resource utilization. Prepare sales forecasts, reports, and business performance presentations for management. Ensure compliance with company policies, commercial procedures, and sub-region regulations. Collaborate with operations, finance, marketing, and supply chain teams to support business objectives. Leadership & Team Development Provide leadership, mentorship, and capability development for sub-region sales teams. Foster a high-performance culture focused on accountability, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Identify talent development and succession planning opportunities within the sales organization. Qualifications & Experience Bachelor’s Degree in Chemical Engineering or Chemistry Minimum 10 - 15 years of sales and business development experience in the chemical industry within multinational environments. Strong exposure with Polymer Dispersions product Proven experience managing sales teams. Strong track record in achieving sales growth and managing key accounts in Fiber Bonding & Adhesive, Paper & Carpet Dispersion Industry is a plus Experience in strategic planning, budgeting, and commercial negotiations. Skills & Competencies Strong leadership and people management skills. Excellent business acumen and strategic thinking. Strong negotiation and stakeholder management capabilities. Excellent communication and presentation skills. Ability to work in a fast-paced, multicultural environment. Strong analytical and problem-solving skills. Working Conditions Frequent regional travel across ASEAN countries is required. Ability to work across different time zones and multicultural environments. description:
Senior Backend Engineer Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Senior Backend Engineer LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now About The Role Within Global Digital Factory ECO (G ‑ ECO/MD), you will contribute to the development of a modern web application as part of an agile DevOps team. The team builds and operates solutions that are jointly used by BASF Coatings and external customers, ensuring high quality, reliability, and continuous improvement throughout the product lifecycle. How You Will Do It - You will be delivering both Intranet and Internet applications to improve BASF Coatings services towards our customers. - Together with your team, you support the process of feature development from the first idea to the final go-live with the end-user. - You will be working in an agile and self-organized way. You not only consider development of new applications but also operations and maintenance as a valuable part of your daily work. - You collaborate closely with product owners, designers, and developers across different locations and time zones communicating asynchronously and ensuring shared understanding of requirements. - You understand, analyze, define, develop, and fulfil quality requirements for planned functionalities in close cooperation with product owners, designers and the development team. - You implement backend (Java Spring Boot) components, ensuring seamless integration with the frontend or other services. You participate in code reviews and uphold coding standards in close cooperation with the team. - You design and implement technical concepts in modern microservice architecture and interact with RESTful APIs. - You write and maintain unit and integration tests to ensure high quality and prevent regressions. - You deploy and test implementations across all environments using CI/CD pipelines and container technologies. - You collaborate on automated testing, monitoring and observability, and support DevOps activities if required. - You document delivered solutions (architecture decisions, API specs, runbooks) to support maintainability and onboarding. - Finally, you keep an eye on current technological developments and apply relevant improvements in daily work. What We Look For - Degree or comparable education in computer or information science, combined with relevant professional experience in an IT environment. - Strong professional experience in Java (8+), Spring, and Spring Boot for backend development. - Practical knowledge of microservice architecture and experience designing, implementing, and consuming RESTful APIs. - Proficiency with SQL databases and basic data ‑ modelling skills. - Hands ‑ on experience with CI/CD pipelines and containerized deployments (Docker; Kubernetes exposure is a plus). - Experience writing unit and integration tests. - Familiarity with DevOps practices and tooling for build, deployment, monitoring, and operational support. - Solid knowledge of Git ‑ based version control (Git, Bitbucket) and common branching / PR workflows. - Comfortable working in agile teams (Scrum or Kanban) and using Agile tools such as JIRA, Confluence, or Azure DevOps. - Strong analytical skills, quick comprehension, problem ‑ solving ability, and an open, collaborative teamwork mindset. - Fluent English for effective communication across locations and time zones. - Understanding of security best practices, identity/authorization concepts (OAuth2/OIDC), and secure coding principles. - Ideally, knowledge of additional Spring modules (Spring Data, Spring Security, Spring Batch, Spring Integration). - Ideally, experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, or GCP) and managed services. name: Senior Specialist Digitalization
description:
Senior Specialist Digitalization Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Senior Specialist Digitalization LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now About The Role Within Global Digital Factory ECO (G‑ECO/MD), you will contribute to the development of a modern web application as part of an agile DevOps team. The team builds and operates solutions that are jointly used by BASF Coatings and external customers, ensuring high quality, reliability, and continuous improvement throughout the product lifecycle. How You Will Do It - You will be delivering both Intranet and Internet applications to improve BASF Coatings services towards our customers. - Together with your team, you support the process of feature development from the first idea to the final go-live with the end-user. - You will be working in an agile and self-organized way. You not only consider development of new applications but also operations and maintenance as a valuable part of your daily work. - You collaborate closely with product owners, designers, and developers across different locations and time zones communicating asynchronously and ensuring shared understanding of requirements. - You understand, analyze, define, develop, and fulfil quality requirements for planned functionalities in close cooperation with product owners, designers and the development team. - You implement both frontend (Angular) and backend (Java Spring Boot) components, ensuring seamless integration and a consistent user experience. You participate in code reviews and uphold coding standards in close cooperation with the team. - You design and implement technical concepts in modern microservice architecture and interact with RESTful APIs. - You develop responsive UI components, integrate them with backend services, and ensure accessibility and performance. - You write and maintain unit and integration tests to ensure high quality and prevent regressions. - You deploy and test implementations across all environments using CI/CD pipelines and container technologies. - You collaborate on automated testing, monitoring and observability, and support DevOps activities if required. - You document delivered solutions (architecture decisions, API specs, runbooks) to support maintainability and onboarding. - Finally, you keep an eye on current technological developments and apply relevant improvements in daily work. What We Look For - Degree or comparable education in computer or information science, combined with relevant professional experience in an IT environment. - Strong professional experience in Java (8+), Spring, and Spring Boot for backend development. - Solid experience with Angular (10+), RxJS, and modern frontend engineering patterns, including component architecture and state management (e.g., NgRx or similar). - Practical knowledge of microservice architecture and experience designing, implementing, and consuming RESTful APIs. - Proficiency with SQL databases (preferably PostgreSQL) and basic data‑modelling skills. - Hands‑on experience with CI/CD pipelines and containerized deployments (Docker; Kubernetes exposure is a plus). - Experience writing unit and integration tests, using JUnit (backend) and common frontend testing frameworks (Jasmine/Karma or Jest). - Familiarity with DevOps practices and tooling for build, deployment, monitoring, and operational support. - Solid knowledge of Git‑based version control (Git, Bitbucket) and common branching / PR workflows. - Comfortable working in agile teams (Scrum or Kanban) and using Agile tools such as JIRA, Confluence, or Azure DevOps. - Strong analytical skills, quick comprehension, problem‑solving ability, and an open, collaborative teamwork mindset. - Fluent English for effective communication across locations and time zones. - Understanding of security best practices, identity/authorization concepts (OAuth2/OIDC), and secure coding principles. description:

We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role To service the customers of the assigned Business Unit and order management Driving Demand Planning and Service Excellence Proactive Customer Orientation Communication Effectiveness Continuous Improvement How Will  You Do It Strategic- Key Activities: Perform sales and delivery order management according to SOP Monitor orders and communicate to customers & Sales team on order status Perform business rules and manage data related to order-to-cash process. Responsible to conduct Monthly S&OP meetings & provide key inputs to management for operations during meeting. Conduct regular analysis of supply chain performance metrics and identify areas for improvement. Coordinate with cross-functional teams i.e. operations, product management & controlling to optimize supply chain processes and resolve any issues or bottlenecks. Stay updated on industry trends, emerging technologies, and regulatory changes impacting the customer service operations or supply chain operations & taking necessary actions wherever required. Prepare reports and presentations to communicate supply chain performance and improvement initiatives to stakeholders. Develop & implement strategies for the disposition of obsolete inventory Analysing & improving supply chain processes & Operations to optimize the cost Operational: Inventory monitoring – local key user of SCII to report DIV, OIV, aging stock Forecast Accuracy – DP FCA reports (external and internal) Logistics Costs – monitor monthly development (filling, packaging, shipping, distribution, demurrage) NCM monthly report – local key user of NCM, report NCM KPIs monthly Delivery Services – local key user of DSC, report DC/DR monthly Respond to verbal (or written) requests in a timely and professional manner, ensuring any promises made are fulfilled Determine the appropriate solution by following operating procedures. Resolve issues without unnecessary delay and escalate more complex problems as required Consistently deliver work tasks on time and to agreed standards. Alert colleagues or manager in advance when timeline or quality of the deliverables is likely to be affected Proactively seek guidance and asks questions to understand structures, processes, systems and responsibilities, and how own role contributes to the broader team goals Creation of Sales Orders based on customer PO or Sales Forecast Tracking of orders, Sales & Customer Receivables on daily basis and highlighting issues if any Connect with Customer on periodic basis Customer AR follow up for payment purpose review credit limit with sales team Follow up with Customer for Call ups against the tender qty and to keep a track of the supplies to avoid oversupply. Forecast and plan the orders  to avoid / minimise delay penalty. Align with Sales colleague and transmit the orders accordingly Focus on digitalisation and utilization of digital tools Co-ordinate with transportation team for vehicle placement & reduced cost for the BU APO forecasting and updating the same in system Inventory control & review of aged inventory What Are We Looking For Education: Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Supply Chain or Logistics Mgt. Working Experience: 5-7 years of relevant experience in Logistics or Supply Chain function Soft Skills: MS Office (Power BI, Excel & Power Point), Proficiency in supply chain management software and tools (e.g., ERP systems, SAP R3, APO DP, SNP). Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with the ability to interpret data and make informed decisions. Excellent organizational and time management abilities to manage multiple priorities and meet deadlines. Proficiency in supply chain management software and tools (e.g., ERP systems, SAP R3, APO DP, SNP). Effective communication and interpersonal skills to collaborate with internal teams and external partners. Knowledge of industry best practices, regulations, and compliance standards in supply chain management. Ability to adapt to changing business needs and work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment. Strong attention to detail and a focus on accuracy in planning and execution. Professional certifications such as Certified Supply Chain Professional (CSCP) or APICS certification are a plus. Well versed with SAP Order To Cash (OTC) processes Good understanding of the products/services of relevant business segments Good knowledge of the business processes Service excellence competencies Ability to collaborate within and outside the team for servicing the customers Work Location Godrej One - Mumbai Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. name: Senior Officer, Order to Cash (Japanese Speaker)
description:
Senior Officer, Order to Cash (Japanese Speaker) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Senior Officer, Order to Cash (Japanese Speaker) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Objectives of the Position The primary function of this role is to provide efficient order management, manage interfaces with customers for order to cash processes and provide improvements to supply chain management. Effectively communicate and resolve order issues with customers and sales. Responsibility of placing replenishment purchase order to supply source and monitor the delivery schedule Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Manage Master Data maintenance process to ensure effective order management and the End-to-End order process by proactively collaborating with multiple functions. Capture market intelligence on the supply chain related topics from daily interaction with customers and share with the business as appropriate and be an integral part of business management. Contribute to development of customer relationships through proactive customer communication related to order management e.g. product availability, order status and delivery tracking. Support Credit Management, Billing and Accounts Receivable Follow-up. Issuing tax invoices for month end closing. Handle customer complaints in BASF’s available system (Non-Conformance Management, tNPS) and actively communicate status, investigation results, corrective actions to customers to ensure on time closure for non-product quality related complaints. Capture customer expectations / requirement and feedback to the business team. Ensure that all operations are carried out in compliant with business rules, internal standards, and local government regulations. Report any areas of concern immediately to Supply Chain Manager. Provide order status report and monitor customers’ order placement against sales forecast and Joint-customer call to customers in case of request by sales team as sales supporter. Job Requirements Bachelor degree in business administration or familiar areas Experience in customer service in an international company for more than 2 years or relevant work experience in SCM/sales/controlling Chemical industry background preferred Technical & Professional Knowledge Experience in using ERP systems such as SAP (at least intermediate level) Experience with data analytics or reporting systems (e.g. PowerBI) is a plus. Understanding of end-to-end OTC process Possess excellent communication, and interpersonal skills Good proficiency in Japanese language and English Proactive, can work independently and in teams About BASF in Malaysia BASF has been present in Malaysia since 1989, and operates a manufacturing plant in Pasir Gudang, Johor. BASF’s Regional Service Hub in Kuala Lumpur provides functional support services to BASF companies in 20 markets across Asia Pacific. BASF also provides infrastructure and utilities support services to its joint ventures BASF PETRONAS Chemicals and Toray BASF Resins at an integrated Verbund site situated in the Gebeng Industrial Zone, Pahang. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Main responsibilities To achieve the sales target in term of cCM1 and volume. Compile and update market information on potential customers, competitor’s activities and market situation. Provide the APO forecast with accuracy and control the goods stock in properly level. Cooperate with Customer Service Operations, Supply Chain Management, Technical Development & Global Key Account Management to support customers with smooth business operation. Record the relevant customer information through Salesforce system (visit report, business opportunities & risks, contact information, customer requirements, samples request, pricing and etc). Drive the innovation and sustainability projects in existing and new customers. Develop and expand the relationship in all level and functions to understand the customer needs in long and short term include pain points. Assure effective volume forecast and continuously improve accuracy. Provide and update the key account plan with short and long terms strategy. Support to achieve teamwork and team spirit for business unit. Additional tasks assign by operational leader. Qualifications Bachelor’s degree or higher in Chemistry, Business, or a related field. At least 5 years of relevant experience in sales or business/market development within the chemical industry, preferably in PU. Strong knowledge of polymers, especially PU. Strong written and spoken English skills, with good computer literacy. Proficient in using Salesforce. Work location Emporium office, Bangkok BASF has been present in Thailand for more than 50 years. The company offers a wide range of products and solutions to support almost all the key industries in the country, including Agricultural Solutions, Intermediates and Process Catalysts, Monomers, Petrochemicals, Coatings, Dispersions and Resins, Performance Chemicals, Care Chemicals, Nutrition and Health, and Performance Materials. BASF in Thailand was established in 1966 and maintains its head office in Bangkok. It operates three production sites in Samutprakarn, Chonburi and Rayong, an Automotive Coatings Technical Competence Center, and a Performance Materials Technical Development Center in Samutprakarn. In 2024, BASF posted sales of €531 million to customers in Thailand and had 623 employees as of the end of the year. Further information is available on www.basf.com/th . description:

We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjectives of the Position: Responsible for ensuring transport management for inbound or outbound (as assigned) are as per stipulated guidelines, procedures and requirements Support process improvement, knowledge and risk management as per overseas delivery services M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities: Outbound Processing transports for packaged deliveries with a focus on sea freight to our customers in compliance with the required customer deadlines at cost-optimal conditions Managing the loading planning & consolidation/deconsolidation activities at the associated companies and external locations or sites, including monitoring of loading times. Legal, internal, safety-relevant and packaging-related regulations must be considered, and customer-specific service levels and other quality requirements must be met Monitoring of all necessary or relevant shipment milestones and ensuring prompt follow ups, updates and correspondences to all relevant stakeholders. Monitoring and assessing the quality of our service providers, transport reliability and feedback rates and tracking shipments & vessels Preparation of all necessary transport documents, control of the proper filing of documents, in particular freight accounting bases, and examination of additional costs incurred Inbound Processing transport for packaged deliveries with a focus on sea freight to our sites within EMEA Managing the loading planning & consolidation/deconsolidation activities at the associated companies and external locations or sites, including monitoring of loading times. Legal, internal, safety-relevant and packaging-related regulations must be considered, and site-specific service levels and other quality requirements must be met Monitoring of all necessary or relevant shipment milestones and ensuring prompt follow ups, updates and correspondences to all relevant stakeholders. Monitoring and assessing the quality of our service providers, transport reliability and feedback rates, and tracking shipments & vessels Preparation and monitoring of all necessary transport documents (e.g verifying draft documents), control of the proper filing of documents, in particular freight accounting bases, and examination of additional costs incurred Job Requirements: Edu c a t i o n: Fresh graduates from programs such as Transport Management, IT, Business Studies, or other relevant fields who are interested in exploring a career in supply chain. Working Experiences 1-2 years in logistics Career starters or first professional experience in transport management Technical & Professional Knowledge Experience in supply chain operations, distribution and warehousing processes Knowledge of the processes of filling and shipping units Experience in handling of MS Office products and, ideally, SAP R/3 quality management and transport management Structured, independent and process-oriented working width High degree of customer orientation High proficiency in spoken and written English The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. name: Intern, Customs Operation
description:
Intern, Customs Operation Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Intern, Customs Operation LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now The main objective of this internship program is to provide students with comprehensive exposure to customs operations within the supply chain, specifically for the chemistry industry. Interns will gain practical understanding of customs-related topics, including HS code classification, analyzing the impact of duties and taxes in international trade, and exploring trade facilities such as free trade agreements, Authorised Economic Operator (AEO), duty drawback etc. Interning with the customs operation team offers valuable insights into international business practices and supply chain dynamics. This experience equips interns with specialized skills that are highly sought after in global trade and logistics, fostering professional growth and enhancing future career opportunities. Learn the basics of customs operations and their critical role in the supply chain. Study foundational customs knowledge and terminology relevant to the chemistry industry. Observe and map out the overview process flow of customs procedures. Understand HS code classification and its importance for accurate documentation and tax calculation. Explore the effects of duties and taxes on international business transactions in the chemistry sector. Gain knowledge about trade facilities, such as free trade agreements, Authorised Economic Operator (AEO), duty drawbacks etc. that benefit global commerce. Participate in improvement initiatives discussion to work on improvement plans and timelines. Participate in small assignments to apply gained knowledge and reinforce learning. description:

We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. 主要工作职责 ： -     安全环保：  严格遵守BPCH的安环保制度    及时指出任何在BPCH内违反安环保制度的行为并汇报确保负责区域环境卫生状况持续改进和提高，确保工完, 料尽,场地清正确处理突发意外事件并在***时间汇报准时参加5分钟安全教育认真进行现场交接班正确使用并维护劳防用品，防护设施和器械 -     生产：  按时完成下达的生产任务和工作量，严格按照操作规程、作业指导书准确操作，实现岗位操作规范化对本人负责的所有与反应或后反应相关的原料配制及投料和转料过程全面负责， 投料前复核原料名称及数量；巡回检查反应状况和物料状况，如有问题及时提出，并寻求解决的方法包装或放料时应做到份量准确、标签准确，整齐划一，桶外表清洁，并确保放料进度及时如实地做好操作记录认真完成相关的工艺清洗及设备的高压清洗，以及设备恢复投料前的密封和检测工作 -     质量和成本：  确保操作卡严格执行并及时准确填写确保及时取样并送分析及时如实汇报质量问题，降低不合格率以及质量有关的客户投诉。生产过程中，巡回检查控制参数合理节约成本，避免资源浪费（原料，公用工程，包装材料，辅助材料）；严格按规定排放废水和其他废弃物。主动积极提出合理化建议 -     设备：  巡回检查设备，及时提出问题并在领班/班长协助下解决检查、保养，整洁和报修所负责的设备根据设备操作程序正确使用设备在领班安排下担当维修监护/协调人,严格执行许可证制度 -     技术:  接受公司组织的各项培训熟悉本岗位各项操作规程与技术，进行一岗多能的学习；能够独立完成本岗位工作学习并拓展本生产线的生产及设备使用技术具备操作与本岗位相关的一些特种设备操作资质 -     纪律:  遵守公司的各项劳动纪律规章制度保持高出勤率，确保完成工作定额。服从 主管/领班的工作安排。在完成本职工作的前提下，主动对其他同事给予工作支持做到与同事有良好地工作关系 。 学历/资质要求: 教育背景:  大专或以上。 专业要求： 化学工程与工艺, 高分子材料与工程, 化工工艺, 应用化工技术,精细化工, 石油化工生产技术等相关专业 工作及经历要求: 化工行业1年以上工作经验。 Competency 要求: 具备相应化工生产操作的技能和知识，能独立进行聚合现场的操作。 请时刻警惕任何可能的招聘欺诈行为！请注意，巴斯夫绝不会在任何情况下向候选人以任何形式收取任何费用。 Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. description:
