Main tasks : Strategic Projects & Analyses To support definition and implementation of segment strategies together with Regional Key Industry Managers (KIMs) To act as Project Management Office (PMO) for regional and local strategy implementation (OKR roll-out and monitoring) To provide regional update of strategic business plan including deviation analysis To develop business cases and facilitate data-driven decision making for strategic initiatives, go-to-market strategies, commercial policies and incentive plans To conduct Performance Analysis: Analyze sales data, market trends and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) to identify growth opportunities and areas for improvement To drive and support cross-country and cross-functional collaboration on key business topics, best practises, ensuring cohesive commercial strategies and execution T o conduct analysis together with Controlling, Human Resources, Product Management to support business management decisions (business development, portfolio management, sales efficiency, etc.) Pricing To develop and communicate pricing guidance as input for countries and their owned price process To increase transparency on raw material cost impacts on finished goods. Margin monitoring and proposal of corrective measures to be communicated towards regional business management To prepare price argumentation material and provide to country sales and KIM/Key Account Management (KAM) organization To monitor implementation of pricing strategy Channel Management To analyze and evaluate the performance of sales channels, identifying opportunities for improvement and ensuring consistent execution across all channels To monitor implementation of channel management project and business rules To develop plan for key strategic distributors for APAC in coordination with Distribution Management function and regional business management Digitalization To represent and drive projects for Digitalization projects within APAC region To act as key focal point for business rules implementation and best practise exchange for key tools (e.g. Salesforce, VISTA, etc.) and to improve data quality in salesforce Qualifications and job requirements : Min. Bachelor's Degree in Engineering, Science, Business Administration, Economics, Chemistry, Industrial Engineering/ Mechanical Engineering, Business Informatics Min. 1- 4 years of professional experience, including internships in similar fields Ideally one with past work experience in the areas of Consulting, Business Development, Strategy, Sales Management and/or Purchasing and those from Chemicals, Pharma, Automotive Industry, Industrial Manufacturing industries will be an added advantage Proficient in MS EXCEL skills - ability to work confidently with large datasets, pivot tables & lookup functions and Macros.  Ideally hands-on knowledge with functions like INDEX‑MATCH, PowerQuery, extensive VBA macros Advanced PowerPoint capabilities, able to translate complex information into clear, structured, management-ready slides and compelling storylines, supported by effective data visualizations (charts, waterfalls, tables and simple dashboards) Ability to break down initiatives into objectives, KPIs, and workstreams; structuring tasks, milestones, and timelines; tracking progress and follow‑ups Strong curiosity about sales effectiveness, market dynamics and customer behaviour Ability to translate a high‑level vision or strategic idea into concrete actions Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. description:
Payroll & People Data Analyst - Mandarin Speaker (Leasing, 12 month contract) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Payroll & People Data Analyst - Mandarin Speaker (Leasing, 12 month contract) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position Seek to understand business needs and provide support, services and solution on payroll, HR data, HR systems and processes. Offer day-to-day support to service recipients on HR inquiries, work collaboratively with others and perform research on payroll and HR data practices by staying abreast of current and emerging market insights and legal legislations in local countries. Participate in process improvements, automation, and digitalization measures to offer best service delivery experience. Main Tasks Operational Excellence Ensure data quality and data integrity for Payroll and HR data processes. Receive, analyze, process and follow-up on personnel administration requests via multiple request channels, ensure accurate and timely interface to the respective internal/ external processing chains i.e. payroll, finance, data analytics, external service providers, local authorities etc. Manage entire termination workflow for all termination types which includes leaves calculations, information checks, letters preparation, systems updates, payment calculations and exit clearance. Responsible for time and attendance administrations, including manual calculations on time allowances/deductions, maintenance of work schedules, leaves, overtimes, and shift substitutions. Support the delivery of payroll inputs and administration i.e. employee subsidies, fleet management, long service awards etc. Support the salary review exercises on employee data preparation, letter generations and new salary processing. Responsible for the local labor administrative activities and scheduled statutory HR reporting, ensure timely and compliant deliveries as per legislation requirements. Responds to recurring or ad-hoc business needs to design and to deliver country specific or customized reports which could not be auto-generated by the analytics/reporting systems. Work closely with other teams and external service providers to ensure all service requests are completed accurately and timely. Service Engagement Providing advisory on employee enquiries, policy/guidelines clarifications, HR processes, HR systems guides, legislation processes etc. Monitor multiples service requests to ensure services delivered by the team are in accordance with established Service Level Agreement (SLA) and internal Key Performance Indicators (KPI). Perform research and information gathering on local market HR news, legislation updates and guidelines to maintain internal knowledge database. Analyze, clarify, and resolve operation issues and enquiries with close collaboration with relevant Country HR and Regional HR Business Partners to ensure quality services are delivered from the service hub. All unsolved and potential operational issues are escalated timely according to establish escalation process, with detailed analysis provided to Assistant Manager and Manager. Process Improvement and Project Initiatives Continuously review and update knowledge documents in areas of Payroll and People data to develop the formation of competence hub. Identify opportunities, prepare and analyze business case, and participate in digitalization and process optimization. Actively work on implementation of process improvements, automation and digitalization measures which will be rolled out predominantly in the GB KL Hub for achieving the efficiency target. Drive initiatives which contribute to the improvement of service experience Identify deviations from the established blueprints of multiple system processors in Payroll and HR data maintenance processes and escalate to Assistant Manager and Manager in ensuring the capturing of business requirements, system settings, configuration needs and change trackers. Provide inputs and participate in user acceptance testing in system maintenance and upgrade, follow up and escalate timely with respective party to ensure fixes or changes can be delivered on time. Team Collaboration Works with moderate supervision within established procedures and practices. Contribute positively to a knowledge sharing environment through generously sharing knowledge with others. Assist in orientation training and act as buddy to new team member. Job Requirements Education Relevant tertiary qualifications: Degree in Accounting, Mathematics, Commerce or Business Administration and Finance Work Experience 1–2 years of experience in Payroll and HR Operations, or fresh graduates with relevant internship experience Proficient in Mandarin to support China-based customers Preferably experience in relevant HR procedures, practices, and country statutory Preferably working knowledge of integrated payroll systems preferably on SAP. Technical & Professional Knowledge Demonstrates a good level of proficiency in language and culture of the supported country/location. Preferably understanding of HR principals and/or Employee Laws of the supported country/location. Strong processing skills and analytical abilities (inclination for Mathematics/Finance/Problem solving). Service mindset with strong interpersonal and communications skills. description:
Assistant Chemist Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Assistant Chemist LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now Objectives of the position: Conduct experimental and theoretical research assignments under the guidance of research scientists. Support innovation processes and scientific work, contributing to continuous improvement in lab operations Main Areas of Responsibilities: Independently perform routine polymer synthesis and analytical tasks using standard techniques, procedures, and equipment. Summarize and document results in electronic lab journals; prepare technical reports. Interpret results and suggest next experimental plan accordingly. Support the innovation process & scientific work by proactively proposing conclusions and making own suggestions, adapting and optimizing methods, procedures and workflow. Prepare, operate, and maintain lab facilities & equipment (e.g., reactors, analytical instruments). Carry out laboratory trials to solve production problems, to modify selected products according to customers’ requirements, to improve production processes and to pre-test production procedures received from BASF Regional prior to up-scaling them into production scale. Perform general lab assignments such as raw material inventory review and lab housekeeping. Analyze raw materials and identify new local sources suitable for product development. Coordinate with procurement, plant or supplier and place raw material purchase order. Carry out administrative work related to development activities and maintain documentation standards. Comply with all safety, health, and environmental regulations, and company requirements. Ensure that the activities do not disturb or cause any problem for the company and for the environment. Ensure required reactions and lab trials are completed on schedule and with accurate results. Support technical sales team on development of new test methods. Build up local market knowledge in formulation/ dispersion to help research to develop new dispersions. Perform Batch card & Catalogue preparation based on experiments. Education: Bachelor’s or master’s degree in chemistry or chemical engineering or Polymer Materials Working Experience: Minimum 2–3 years of professional experience in polymer dispersion R&D, production operations, or chemistry lab activities. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Team relevant chemistry, and/or methods and/or technology (= Solid knowledge of subject and processes (can prepare, perform and analyze independently)). Team relevant technical equipment (= Solid knowledge of subject and processes (can prepare, perform and analyze independently)). Polymer science background preferred Languages: English (= Advanced basic knowledge (spoken & written for standard information)). Most relevant technical competencies: EHS Awareness (level 2) Data & IP Security Awareness (level 2) Ability to analyze results and to solve technical problems (level 1) Ability to organize tasks and set priorities (level 2) Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Objectives of the Position • Provide Technical Advisory at respective region through the area of part design, processing, tooling and material in order to achieve manufacturing excellent part performance quality at customers in our Engineering Plastics business. • To optimize product/material processing under trouble shooting at customer location to target excellent part quality. • According to the agreed strategies, policies and practices, provides input and participates in business development and planning, product / processing development and modification, customer intimacy to support the implementation of sales strategies and policies in China at defined sales goal. • Support Application Development and Product Launch initiatives in South China for Engineering Plastics together with commercial team in Project Acquisition and Commercialization for sales Growth. Segment strategy • Collaborates with marketing team to build the road map of each segment of consumer electronics, new energy and automotive, also implementing the road map to contribute the business development • Supports in the development of the regional / global product/ segment strategy, in alignment with Sales and Product Management. Innovations • Facilitates innovation activities and participates at innovation events (such as Customer TechDays) • Works closely with the sales teams to promote new products and innovative solutions to customers. • Organizes and conducts technical presentations and seminars What We Look For • Bachelor or Master's Degree in Polymer, Chemistry, Material Science, Chemical Engineering, Material Engineering, Mechanical Engineering or equivalent field. • 5-8 years of working experience preferably either in Engineering Plastics especially in the areas of sales and or market or application development or in other relevant industries. • Strong ability to identify opportunities or problem, and formulate and implement strategy or actions accordingly. • Strong in establishing & fostering customer relationships. • Strong sales & marketing background & business experience. • Good conflict management and good networker. • Proven track record of developing and executing successful commercial strategies and plans throughout an organization. • Good knowledge of Engineering Plastic. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. O bjectives of the Position: Responsible for ensuring transport management for inbound or outbound (as assigned) are as per stipulated guidelines, procedures and requirements Support process improvement, knowledge and risk management as per overseas delivery services M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities: Outbound Processing transports for packaged deliveries with a focus on sea freight to our customers in compliance with the required customer deadlines at cost-optimal conditions Managing the loading planning & consolidation/deconsolidation activities at the associated companies and external locations or sites, including monitoring of loading times. Legal, internal, safety-relevant and packaging-related regulations must be considered, and customer-specific service levels and other quality requirements must be met Monitoring of all necessary or relevant shipment milestones and ensuring prompt follow ups, updates and correspondences to all relevant stakeholders. Monitoring and assessing the quality of our service providers, transport reliability and feedback rates and tracking shipments & vessels Preparation of all necessary transport documents, control of the proper filing of documents, in particular freight accounting bases, and examination of additional costs incurred Inbound Processing transport for packaged deliveries with a focus on sea freight to our sites within EMEA Managing the loading planning & consolidation/deconsolidation activities at the associated companies and external locations or sites, including monitoring of loading times. Legal, internal, safety-relevant and packaging-related regulations must be considered, and site-specific service levels and other quality requirements must be met Monitoring of all necessary or relevant shipment milestones and ensuring prompt follow ups, updates and correspondences to all relevant stakeholders. Monitoring and assessing the quality of our service providers, transport reliability and feedback rates, and tracking shipments & vessels Preparation and monitoring of all necessary transport documents (e.g verifying draft documents), control of the proper filing of documents, in particular freight accounting bases, and examination of additional costs incurred Job Requirements: Edu c a t i o n: Fresh graduates from programs such as Transport Management, IT, Business Studies, or other relevant fields who are interested in exploring a career in supply chain. W o r k in g Experience: 1-2 years in logistics Career starters or first professional experience in transport management T e c hn i c al & Professional Knowledge: Experience in supply chain operations, distribution and warehousing processes Knowledge of the processes of filling and shipping units Experience in handling of MS Office products and, ideally, SAP R/3 quality management and transport management Structured, independent and process-oriented working width High degree of customer orientation High proficiency in spoken and written English The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Objectives of the Position The Purchase To Pay provides invoice verification services for Inbound / Outbound supply chain activities. It is the focal point for the relevant invoice verification activities before payment is initiated to the service providers. The analyst, will execute the process with a consistent high level of service to be able to meet the growing needs of the businesses in Asia Pacific Main Tasks Operations and Performance: Check and verify inbound and outbound invoices against contracted / approved Spot rates from service providers according to guidelines Coordinate with service providers / local country Supply Chain / Finance teams for invoice clarifications and corrections to avoid service interruptions Provide full and accurate supporting documents to Finance team for vendor payment Understand charging mechanism and process for relevant Supply Chain services Participate proactively in new workflows or process improvements for on time and accurate verification services Identify risk related to the process and propose mitigations Troubleshooting and problem solving Support and provide documents for internal and external audit purpose Requirements Bachelor degree in Business or Supply Chain Management or Finance and Accounting. 2-4 years relevant experience in Purchase to pay, supply chain management/ shipping management, including hands-on experience in dealing with logistics service provider and shipping invoice handling. Supply Chain Management or Finance and Accounting /process knowledge, SAP Transportation Management experience, including hands-on experience in operational shipping / logistics management, good communication skills. BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre was established in 2005. Located in Kuala Lumpur, the regional service hub has approximately 1,100 employees and growing. Focused on being a competency and digitalization driven hub, it also provides quality functional and know-how services in the areas of finance, controlling, human resources, real estate and facilities management, communications, environmental, health and safety, procurement, import-export order management, and information services to BASF companies and affiliates in the Asia Pacific region. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay that cover the Europe and Americas regions. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Around 111,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. About The Role Ensure all QM rules in accordance with ISO9001 / IATF16949 / ISO14001 / ISO 45001 / VDA / AIAG procedures Address and resolve quality issues, providing technical input and guidance on process enhancements and improvements at suppliers Drive pro-active QM mindset in BU How You Will Do It Cooperate with Sales and Engineer team on product development and jointly define process parameters and criteria to ensure supplier process capability is effective to meet product and process requirements (Focus on critical products, critical processes or critical suppliers) Work with global team to improve Supplier Quality Management system Drive “Quality Assurance Agreements” (QSV) with Suppliers and execute accordingly Supplier evaluation is based on historical performance together with purchasing and release score to supplier regularly. Supplier audit, including potential supplier audit, annual audit, product and process audit and assess their practices and procedures and identify areas for improvement Take responsibility for the overall quality activities concerning quality assurance of purchased parts, monitor supplier performance, validate supplier 8D report and collect failure cost to support procurement for compensation Project supplier nomination procedure engagement based on supplier quality and capacity assessment. Check and approve product drawing and release to supplier Project APQP support for supplier part, regarding OTS and PPAP work, project audit for supplier. Support on supplier development based on customer and business requirements. Apply quality tools e.g. Cp/Cpk, SPC, R&R, 8D to analyze and improve supplier quality performance Supplier changes are managed and approved based on quality, cost savings, and customer requirements. Cooperate with the other QM functions to improve customer satisfaction Work together with delegated procurement to transfer customer CSR to supplier. Assist in maintaining Quality Management (QM) documents in accordance with ISO 9001-2015 and IATF 16949-2016 standards. This involves ensuring compliance with the quality management system by keeping all necessary documentation and records up to date and accurate. QMS Audit Records: Maintain comprehensive records of both external and internal quality management system audits to demonstrate compliance and facilitate continuous improvement. Assist in compiling customer-specific QMS requirements, including supporting the implementation and maintenance of customer special Quality status related requirements. Participate in Manufacturing Site Assessment audits, monitor and maintain periodic customer audit scores, and fulfill other customer-specific quality requirements. Oversee compliance with the Supplier Quality Assurance Manual. What We Look For Bachelor or above on Mechanical or Production Engineering. At least 6 years of experience in quality functions, including supplier quality and project quality with automotive sector. Certificated auditor for IATF16949, VDA 6.3, VDA6.5 or equivalent knowledge. Familiar with quality tools such as APQP, PPAP, 8D, 5why, SPC, FMEAs, etc. Knowledge of injection molding processes, Casting, Machining, and GD&T is preferred. Ability to work under pressure. Good skills in communication and negotiation. Flexible on business trip Work Location Godrej One - Mumbai Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. name: Trade Compliance Operations AP (Senior Executive)
description:
Trade Compliance Operations AP (Senior Executive) Job Details | BASF SE Skip to main content Find jobs Language Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) Employee Login Job Description Trade Compliance Operations AP (Senior Executive) LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS LOCATION COMPANY JOB FIELD JOB TYPE JOB ID FLEXIBLE WORK OPTIONS Apply now We are a leading chemical company, with the best teams developing intelligent solutions for our customers, and for a sustainable future. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Objectives of the Position •    Support BASF group companies to meet all legal and regulatory requirements related to trade compliance. •    Protect BASF’s license to operate by ensuring adherence to Import and export control, domestic regulations, sanctions, and screening obligations. Main Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities Execute trade compliance related activities for BASF companies to ensure compliance. Ensure timely update in Daily Operation Reports and regularly review compliance performance with relevant areas against business processes predefined criteria. Evaluate, analyze and verify trade compliance regulatory data and documentation to support system accuracy, database integrity, and ongoing compliance monitoring. Support team members in resolving issues related to service delivery including communication with internal customers / stakeholders / other business partners. Perform necessarily follow up action to ensure deliverables completed in a timely manner. Ensure that BASF’s trade compliance processes and standards are effectively applied across Asia Pacific (and other supported regions), covering import, export, and regulatory requirements. This includes monitoring regulatory changes, assessing business impact, managing stakeholders, and implementing compliance activities using BASF tools and digital solutions. Facilitate knowledge exchange on best practices sharing and training, including participation in regional or cross-regional projects and initiatives Report preparation, including KPI reporting, cost charging, non-conformance monitoring and improvement initiatives, using BASF tools, Visual Dashboard, and digital solutions. Act as focal point to support internal and external audits, including conducting Quality Checks to identify potential improvements and implement preventive measures. Ensure timely review, update and continuous improvement of work documentation such as process descriptions, work instructions to maintain sustainable knowledge retention and process standardization within department. Interface with various stakeholders such as Supply Chain, Customer Services and Technical Experts to enable effective implementation of compliance policies and BASF minimum requirements. Drive continuous improvement through Lean Six Sigma methodologies and digitalization initiatives to enhance operational excellence   and process efficiency. Execute assigned processes with a consistently high standard of service to effectively meet the growing demands of business operations across relevant regions. Guide and support team members in performing routine activities for Trade Compliance Ops AP team. Commit to continuous self-development through proactive learning and development (70:20:10 approach) and actively contribute to building team capability. Job Requirements Education Bachelor’s degree in International Business, Law, Supply Chain Management, Logistics, or a related field. Background in Chemistry or related science is an added advantage for product-related compliance. Working Experience Minimum 3 years’ experience in regulatory or compliance functions, product safety & stewardship, supply chain /order management operations, or procurement activities within a multinational environment is preferred. Practical experience with import and export processes, including documentation and custom procedures, is an added advantage. Technical & Professional Knowledge Knowledge of regulatory controls, import/export requirements, license/permit management, and trade compliance procedures and standards in Asia Pacific countries. Understanding global trade regulations (e.g. export control, sanctions, restricted party screening) would be an advantage. Proficiency in MS Office 365 (Excel /Power Point essential) Experience with SAP systems in supporting compliance or logistics processes would be beneficial. Fluent in English (written and spoken); proficiency in Chinese or German is an asset. Experience with digital tools and automation technologies (e.g., Robotic Process Automation, Power BI) or Lean Six Sigma methodologies. Analytical thinking, problem-solving, organizational (coordination) skills Effective communication and interpersonal skills. High attention to detail, proactive mindset, and able to work independently   as well as collaboratively in a team environment. The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States. Further information at www.basf.com . Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. Our total offer includes a

Main Task Mid-long term Strategy development Manage BU’s strategy development including strategic alliance with competitors, tolling partners and distributors, possible M&A and monitor the implementation to achieve the strategic goal. Target and budget planning Drive the business of assigned customers/territory aiming to meet or exceed business targets (cCM1 & Volumes) for respective customers and another financial target as DSO, DIV and Fixed cost. This includes targets for customer’s transplants outside of Japan. Market development management Drive all activities and develop market development plan (channel management, new business development with new or existing products, technology, innovation, new services) to achieve the defined goal. Key Customer and Industry management Drive to build strong strategic relationships with customers and industries. Taking all responsibilities of defined regional and global Japanese key account activities including their transplant activities outside of Japan. External communication Manage external customer and market communication professionally such as product launching, deletion, product safety and registration, advertising, promotion programs, seminars and workshops in cooperation. People and organizational development Develop long term sustainable organizational and people development of BUs in line with the company objectives and coach internal stakeholders. Drive X-BU/X-Country activities Internal Commit to drive X-BU activities including Japanese transplant activities for the achievement of Japan long term objectives and vision. It includes to provide a view of upstream chemical knowledge to entire BJL. Minimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position • Education: Bachelor above. Advantages for majoring chemistry, business management • Working Experience: Advantage with experience in commodity chemical sales • Technical & Professional knowledge: Business level in English and good knowledge of sales & marketing, SCM and finance. ■ 職務概要 日本市場における売上目標および中長期戦略の達成に向け、社内外の関係者と連携しながら営業活動および市場開発を推進していただきます。 顧客・市場への提案活動だけでなく、戦略立案から実行までを主体的にリードいただくポジションです。 ■ 主な業務内容 1. 中長期戦略の立案・推進 事業戦略の策定および実行の推進 パートナー企業・販売代理店とのアライアンス検討 必要に応じたM&A案件の検討・推進 2. 売上・予算管理 顧客または担当領域における売上・収益目標の達成 予算管理および財務指標のモニタリング 3. 市場開発・ビジネス開拓 新規ビジネス・新市場開拓の推進 製品、技術、サービスを活用した市場拡大施策の立案・実行 チャネル戦略の構築・推進 4. 顧客・業界マネジメント 主要顧客との関係構築および強化 グローバル顧客対応（海外展開含む） 業界動向の分析およびビジネス機会の特定 5. 対外コミュニケーション 製品発売、プロモーション、セミナー等の企画・運営 顧客および市場への情報発信 製品安全・規制対応に関するコミュニケーション 6. 組織・人材開発 組織の持続的成長に向けた人材育成 社内関係者へのコーチング・ナレッジ共有 7. クロスリージョン／クロスBU連携 海外拠点や他部門との連携によるプロジェクト推進 グローバル視点でのビジネス拡大支援 ■ 応募資格 必須要件 大卒以上 ビジネスレベルの英語力 営業・マーケティング、サプライチェーン、財務に関する基礎知識 歓迎要件 化学業界または関連業界での営業経験 化学またはビジネス系専攻の学位 Apply now A unique total offer: you@BASF At BASF you get more than just compensation. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Main Responsibilities: Carry out maintenance activities in accordance with maintenance and EHS work instructions to avoid causing issues for the company, the environment, and employees.Implement workplace safety policies and standards. Serve as part of the SERT team. Responsible for maintenance activities including preventive maintenance (PM) and repairs. Responsible for troubleshooting equipment damage and plant equipment breakdowns. Develop repair plans and spare parts procurement plans. Act as a trainer within the maintenance department. Responsible for creating or revising documents such as procedures, work instructions (WI), lists, and forms for the maintenance department. Evaluate the work performance of employees or contractors to ensure the quality of work delivered. Responsible for monthly and annual reports of the maintenance department. Record and monitor progress and reports of plant equipment breakdowns. Source, select, and negotiate the procurement of materials, spare parts, and services from vendors in collaboration with procurement. Responsible for the negotiation process and execution of work carried out by vendors. Plan and execute CAPEX projects and changes based on Management of Change (MOC). Execute and close audit and plant inspection findings. Responsible for GR (Goods Receipt), GI (Goods Issue), and stock-taking activities in the maintenance warehouse. Carry out maintenance work instructions assigned by superiors. Act as Deputy Maintenance Manager. Job Requirements: Work Experience: Bachelor’s degree in mechanical/electrical engineering with minimum 7 years of experience. Technical & Professional Skills: Have technical knowledge in electrical and mechanical systems Familiarity in schematics PID, CSMS, CMMS Able to plan, prioritize and organize work and ensure adequate Effective communication and people management skills Strong understanding of RBM, ISO 9001, and project management Knowledge of technical drawing, PID and measurement tools. We connect and develop people with diverse talents all over the world. For you, this means a variety of ways to progress. Not only your performance but also your personality matter to us, regardless of gender, age, ethnicity, orientation, and background.  At BASF, careers develop from opportunities. Main responsibilities Oversee the end-to-end procurement cycle for indirect materials and/or services, ensuring alignment with business objectives. Establish and maintain strong relationships with stakeholders and suppliers, lead sourcing initiatives, and promote digitalization to enhance process efficiency. Manage risks, facilitate supplier development, and conduct negotiations to ensure sustainable and reliable supply chains. Serve as the Single Point of Contact (SPOC) for site- or country-specific procurement for smaller sites or contribute as a member of a dedicated cluster team. Collaborate closely with procurement teams and internal partners on projects spanning the value chain, representing Procurement as a valued and equal partner within cross-cluster sourcing teams. Demonstrate adaptability in response to evolving business needs, including involvement in Project Procurement Management and digitalization initiatives. Qualifications University degree, preferably in chemicals / Business / Economics or relevant. Minimum 5 years working experience and procurement experience is a plus Working at the business organization at a multi-group company for chemical industry is preferred. Proficiency in both spoken and written English. Good communication skills, ability to lead by influence, convincing skills. name: EHS, ED, Yokkaichi-Rokuromi Plant 求人詳細
description:
EHS, ED, Yokkaichi-Rokuromi Plant 求人詳細 | BASF SE 目次にスキップ 求人を検索 言語 Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) 従業員ログイン 求人内容 EHS, ED, Yokkaichi-Rokuromi Plant 勤務地 会社 ジョブフィールド 職種 ジョブID 柔軟な働き方の選択肢 勤務地 会社 ジョブフィールド 職種 ジョブID 柔軟な働き方の選択肢 今すぐ応募する O bjectives of the Position Why does this position exist?  With what permanent objectives? Following the guidance of the site manager and relevant company policies, standard and requirement, plan, coordinate and initiate all the EHS related activities in Rokuromi, which is including implementation of new standard from regional or global HQ and required laws. According to the EHS mandatory requirements and Responsible Care ® principles and practices, lead the implementation of the EHS programs to ensure the compliance of company, corporate and mandatory requirements; and assure management’s initiatives in taking necessary actions to raise employee awareness of risks and to reduce, avoid and protect employee from hazards and occupational illnesses in the workplace M ain Areas of Responsibilities and Key Activities (EHS) : Interpret and communicate the applicable company, corporate and mandatory EHS regulations and requirements to the line management, including recommendation on implementation and improvement actions to support meeting the safety and environmental protection standards. Responsible for RC Code 4, 6 & 7. According to the guideline, requirement from corporate EHS, lead to implement the relevant safety and environment protection policies and procedures including enforcing the observation of all company’s operations, safety, ecology and housekeeping guidelines to maintain a working environmental that supports work safety and production activities. Having high mindset regarding EHS and guide team members as a member of RC committee at site. Implement the agreed company safety standards and preventive guidelines by working with other managers to identify the common safety/ health hazards inherent in the company’s existing and future operations processes covering production, transportation, warehousing work processes/ product etc to enforce EHS implementation to eliminate the damages related to industrial hazards. Review new/ existing products, equipment, and processes for potential hazards and recommend to the site manager and other managers modifications. Consolidate the training needs to help identify the EHS training/education programs, schedules and target groups to strengthen the employees’ awareness on EHS. May need to conduct training/workshop, introduction programs. Supervise the process to organize the education/ training programs to ensure the delivery and follow-up on the course evaluations to help decision-making on the use of such program. Need to supervise issuing regular newsletter to collect EHS suggestions, concerns and informing employees, the EHS practices. Identify and interpret applicable EHS regulations to recommend priorities and improvement actions, including developing safe work practices and measures with line personnel to monitor conformance through safety and housekeeping inspections. Organize the audits of EHS performance with the necessary BASF or external partners and initially consolidate/summarize the performance reports in order to ensure the correct and consistent implementation of the EHS requirements/regulations, and alert line management the detected problem areas. According to the company requirements, lead the investigation of occupational accidents or problem areas to recommend improvement/ preventive measures. Establish and maintain a documentation system to keep company, corporate and mandatory EHS rules/ regulations, communication materials, inspection and audit reports/ materials etc. to ensure that the major activities are properly documented according to needs. Initially prepare EHS statistics, analysis and reports (e.g. accidents, training times) for internal and external (BASF, government authorities, third party auditor where applicable) use to help monitor the conformance of company/ corporate/ mandatory EHS requirements Job Requirements (m inimum requirement to carry out the work) Edu c a t i o n Bachelor degree of Chemistry related or equivalent W o r k in g Experience Minimum 5 years in EHS or production or E&M field T e c hn i c a l & Professional Knowledge Technical & Professional Knowledge on EHS 今すぐ応募する A unique total offer: you@BASF BASFでは、報酬だけではなく、社員それぞれのライフステージを支援するため、育成プログラムや福利厚生制度など様々なサポートを提供しています。詳しくはこちら。 A

name: Manufacturing Manager, ED, Mie-Rokuromi Plant 求人詳細
description:
Manufacturing Manager, ED, Mie-Rokuromi Plant 求人詳細 | BASF SE 目次にスキップ 求人を検索 言語 Ceština (Ceská republika) Dansk (Danmark) Deutsch (Deutschland) Ελληνικά (Ελλάδα) English (United States) Español (España) Español (México) Français (France) Magyar (Magyarország) Italiano (Italia) 日本語 (日本) 한국어 (대한민국) Norsk bokmål ‎(Norwegian Bokmål) Nederlands (België / Nederland) Polski (Polska) Português (Brasil) Português (Portugal) Slovencina (Slovensko) Svenska (Konungariket Sverige) ภาษาไทย (ประเทศไทย) Türkçe (Türkiye) 简体中文 (中国大陆) 繁體中文(台灣) 従業員ログイン 求人内容 Manufacturing Manager, ED, Mie-Rokuromi Plant 勤務地 会社 ジョブフィールド 職種 ジョブID 柔軟な働き方の選択肢 勤務地 会社 ジョブフィールド 職種 ジョブID 柔軟な働き方の選択肢 今すぐ応募する Objectives of the Position This role is responsible for designing and installing optimal equipment in collaboration with the Engineering & Maintenance (E&M) team to support efficient and safe plant operations. It also involves training plant personnel to perform self-maintenance of equipment. Under the guidance of the Site Manager and in accordance with company policies, the role oversees production, quality, shipping, and receiving activities to ensure monthly sales targets are met with high quality and cost efficiency. Additionally, the role manages both existing and new biological wastewater treatment facilities, working closely with external partners and authorities to ensure environmental compliance. Main Responsibilities 1. Deputy Site Manager Duties Acts as the second deputy site manager when both the Site Manager and QA/Technical Manager are absent (excluding decisions on management reviews, long-term investments, HR matters, and budgeting). 2. Safety & Environmental Management Implements safety and environmental policies based on corporate and legal requirements. Conducts first aid training for all site employees and monitors safety attitudes. Serves as an Emergency Response Team member, leading on-site actions during emergencies and reporting to the central ER team. Leads EHS-related activities from a technical perspective as part of the Responsible Care (RC) committee. Identifies and improves unsafe working conditions in collaboration with the production team. 3. Production Management Develops production plans based on sales forecasts and R&D requirements. Coordinates procurement of raw materials and packaging. Revises production schedules in response to equipment issues. Participates in management meetings and collaborates with other departments to ensure smooth operations. Evaluates production performance, cost, and delivery timelines. Assesses feasibility of new product manufacturing and recommends necessary changes. Leads weekly production meetings and conducts regular site inspections. Oversees purchasing, inventory control, and interplant material coordination. 4. Waste Management Operates and supervises wastewater treatment facilities, ensuring water quality standards are met. Communicates with external partners and authorities regarding environmental compliance. Manages solid waste handling and audits waste disposal companies. 5. Equipment Design & Installation Plans and executes installation, relocation, and upgrades of machinery using new technologies. Designs, estimates costs, and installs electrical and instrumentation equipment. Manages individual investment projects from design to commissioning. Collaborates with local and regional technical teams to ensure equipment suitability. 6. Maintenance & Planning Develops maintenance schedules and spare parts inventory based on production needs. Participates in technical and process improvement planning. 7. Team & People Management Plans team structure, headcount, and budget. Coaches and evaluates team members, fostering skill development and succession planning. Reviews team performance and identifies areas for improvement. Implements strategies to enhance productivity, quality, and efficiency. Job Requirements University degree or equivalent Minimum 10 years of experience in plant operations or related fields Over 3 years of experience in people and team management Business-level English proficiency Native-level Japanese proficiency 今すぐ応募する A unique total offer: you@BASF BASFでは、報酬だけではなく、社員それぞれのライフステージを支援するため、育成プログラムや福利厚生制度など様々なサポートを提供しています。詳しくはこちら。 A

Provide solutions to business operations proactively by analyzing / reviewing related OTC operations and increase continuous efficiency and improvement with guidance. Monitor/analyze SCM KPIs with transparency and execute improvement activities with guidance. Execute actions that contribute to achieving sales, profit, and growth targets by offering services that meet customer needs with guidance. Contribute to SCM related and integration projects with guidance. Core Responsibility Strategy Contribute to EM strategy with responsible area. Ensures strategy can provide efficient, agile and reliable customer services that are aligned with EM strategies as well as BASF’s regional and local strategies, with guidance by other team members and manager. Customer relationship management Manage customer relationships in accordance with related business unit, and sales strategies, e.g. perspectives, sales excellence, value based management, key account management, and business rules. Order to Cash and Non-conformance management Provide order management-related expertise and information to CSO team and functional counterparts for continuous process improvement, system harmonization and solution coordinator for process problem solving. Capture customer expectation / requirement in NCM process within own responsible business area. Analyze the root causes, propose corrective measures, and ensure on-time closure of the end-to-end process in coordination with BUs and Functions for non-product quality complaints. Key Performance Indicators Monitor core KPIs and identify potential improvements. Contribute for improving performance within EM. Process Improvement Identify and implement improvements in related process areas and share best practices within own team. Projects Contribute mid to small size project with defined/agreed schedule and contribute budget control for Supply Chain related projects within EM as a project member. Monitoring Logistics Service Providers Services Responsible for monitoring appropriate LSP services, include compliance, on time delivery to meet business requirement. Training and development Actively participate any relevant trainings. Minimum Education and Qualification Required for the Position Education: Bachelor's Degrees. Ability to speak English as basic level are required. Working Experience: Two (2) years in supply chain, especially customer services area or Four (4) years in other areas with certain expertise, which are closer to customer services. Working knowledge of SAP or other ERP system required, and MS Office is also required. Technical & Professional Knowledge: Developed in project management with an ability to effectively negotiate with commercial, functional units, and regional units, thorough knowledge of sales process and OTC process required. 今すぐ応募する A unique total offer: you@BASF BASFでは、報酬だけではなく、社員それぞれのライフステージを支援するため、育成プログラムや福利厚生制度など様々なサポートを提供しています。詳しくはこちら。 A

Objectives of the Position Contribute to the continuous development of BASFs´ Standard for Trade Compliance by understanding the Regulatory requirement across Trade Compliance disciplines and contextualizing for business context. Provide Global oversight and act as liaison with Regional Trade Compliance leads regarding National Trade Compliance Officer activities in each jurisdiction. Main Tasks Continuously develop BASF’s´ Standard for Trade Compliance across disciplines and contextualizing for different Business scenarios. Collaborate with Trade Compliance Global Topic Owners e.g. Responsible Supply Chain, Embargos, Dual-use and Defense Military Compliance, Chemicals Trade Control to co-develop BASF’s approach to new or changing regulation. Critically evaluate compliance concepts developed by Topic Owners and assess implementation effectiveness for all BASF legal entities in the light of dynamic trade restrictions and varied business models. Support Global Topic Owners and Trade Compliance service unit leaders in designing practical implementation of effective checks and controls in business processes Develop & enhance risk assessment framework, template and model(s) that can be used in different cases, in alignment with Corporate Centre Business Model / Business Partner Consultancy Assess impact for EU sanctions packages from Business Partner perspective Support business units or service units with deep-dive evaluations on business partners upon request Liaison for ad hoc requests related to suspicious business partners, e.g., from Corporate Trade Compliance, Treasury, Corporate Legal or other functions with regard to Anti-Money-Laundering, German Supply Chain Act, Modern Slavery etc. Global oversight of National Trade Compliance activities and strengthen BASF’s line of defense in all jurisdictions Act as focal point to understand National Trade Compliance activities, compliance status, local Authority requirements in all jurisdictions & countries Assist Regional Trade Compliance leads in collecting and consolidating Executive-level reports on Trade Compliance landscape and activities in each jurisdiction, ensuring timeliness and quality of content Identify areas for improvement in Regulatory monitoring and compliance, and participate in solution design Identify areas for improvement in processes, including Communication & Awareness, and participate in solution design Develop a repository of Regulations including update frequency, access rights, viewer rights, relevant content and lifecycle management (eg Archiving) Enhance the Audit management and Incident management platform Requirements Education University graduate in business law, legal studies, international trade, Business/ Economics with specialization in Trade Compliance/Regulatory certification is a plus Working Experience Min 8 years of relevant professional experience in the field of foreign trade or compliance within banking sector, Manufacturing chemicals/ pharmaceutical/ life science industry, commodities trading, or international trading company Experience in both service industry AND goods-based industry preferred Experience in managing risks in a multi-jurisdictional business environment Technical & Professional Knowledge Demonstrable analytical and conceptual thinking Excellent deductive reasoning, critical thinking, and problem-solving skills Entrepreneurial drive to take ownership until resolution Able to influence without direct authority Demonstrated track record of multi-country, cross-functional coordination and/or project management Familiarity with BASF business processes would be a plus The BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur (BASF Asia-Pacific Service Centre Sdn Bhd) is strategically located in the capital city of Malaysia. Since its inception in 2005, the hub has expanded from 130 employees to over 1,300 employees currently and is expected to keep growing. As an ISO 9001:2005 certified organization, the Service Hub Kuala Lumpur is committed to delivering high quality expertise and services in finance, people, supply chain, EHSQ (environment, health, safety, quality), product safety and digital. We take pride in providing our business services to BASF companies and affiliates across 20 markets in the Asia Pacific region and beyond. Moreover, we offer our services in 8 different languages to cater to the diverse needs of our stakeholders. Our focus on competency and technology-driven solutions allows us to consistently deliver exceptional services, thereby contributing to the success of our businesses. BASF also has two other regional hubs located in Berlin, Germany, and Montevideo, Uruguay, which cover the European and American regions. BASF Service Hub Kuala Lumpur was recognized with nine prestigious awards in 2024, reflecting its commitment to creating a vibrant, inclusive, and empowering workplace. These awards include winning the title of Operational Excellence in HR at the GBS Asia Awards 2024, multiple accolades at the Life at Work Awards 2024, where the hub was recognized as First Runner-up for Workplace Excellence (International Organization) and CEO Champion (International Organization), as well as Second Runner-up for Best International Organization. The hub also won the title for Excellence in Learning Tech Advancement System and a Certificate of Excellence in Talent Management and Leadership at the HRD Awards 2024. Furthermore, the hub secured a Bronze Award for Excellence in Digital Transformation at the HR Excellence Awards 2024 and received a Bronze Award for Human Resources Impact and an Honorary Award for Creative Talent Management Impact at the SSON Impact Awards Asia 2024. At BASF, we create chemistry for a sustainable future. Our ambition: We want to be the preferred chemical company to enable our customers’ green transformation. We combine economic success with environmental protection and social responsibility. Around 112,000 employees in the BASF Group contribute to the success of our customers in nearly all sectors and almost every country in the world. Our portfolio comprises, as core businesses, the segments Chemicals, Materials, Industrial Solutions, and Nutrition & Care; our standalone businesses are bundled in the segments Surface Technologies and Agricultural Solutions. BASF generated sales of €65.3 billion in 2024. BASF shares are traded on the stock exchange in Frankfurt (BAS) and as American Depositary Receipts (BASFY) in the United States.
